def_en: To formally give up power or responsibility
quote_en: The king decided to abdicate the throne in favor of his eldest son.
quote_zh: 国王决定将王位让给长子。
ex_en: Do not abdicate your own judgment to the loudest voice in the room; think for yourself.
ex_zh: 不要把你自己的判断让给房间里最响亮的声音；独立思考。

def_en: To regard with disgust and hatred
quote_en: I abhor cruelty to animals and always speak out against it.
quote_zh: 我憎恶虐待动物的行为，并且总是公开反对。
ex_en: To abhor an injustice is natural; to act against it is heroic.
ex_zh: 痛恨不公是自然的；与之抗争则是英勇的。

quote_en: The idea of cheating on an exam is abhorrent to honest students.
quote_zh: 考试作弊的想法对诚实的学生来说是可恶的。
ex_en: An abhorrent act becomes even worse when it is done in the name of righteousness.
ex_zh: 以正义之名行可恶之事，则更加恶劣。

def_en: Something that causes disgust or hatred
quote_en: The slave trade was an abomination that should never be forgotten.
quote_zh: 奴隶贸易是一种永远不应被遗忘的令人憎恶的事物。
ex_en: An abomination to one culture may be a sacred tradition to another; seek understanding before judgment.
ex_zh: 一种文化中的令人憎恶之物可能是另一种文化中的神圣传统；在判断之前先寻求理解。

def_en: An original inhabitant of a land, especially Australia
quote_en: The aborigine shared ancient stories about the land and its spirits.
quote_zh: 那位原住民分享了关于这片土地及其神灵的古老故事。
ex_en: Every aborigine carries a library of knowledge that no textbook can replace.
ex_zh: 每一位原住民都携带着任何教科书都无法替代的知识宝库。

def_en: To stop or cancel something before completion
quote_en: The mission had to be aborted due to a technical failure.
quote_zh: 由于技术故障，任务不得不中止。
ex_en: Do not abort a good plan just because of one small obstacle; find a way around it.
ex_zh: 不要因为一个小小的障碍就中止一个好计划；想办法绕过它。

def_en: To exist in large numbers or great quantity
quote_en: During spring, wildflowers abound in the meadows.
quote_zh: 春天，草地上开满了野花。
ex_en: Opportunities abound for those who prepare themselves, not for those who wait.
ex_zh: 机会为那些做好准备的人大量存在，而不是为那些等待的人。

quote_en: His abrasive tone offended everyone in the meeting.
quote_zh: 他粗暴的语气冒犯了会议中的每个人。
ex_en: An abrasive truth is still better than a smooth lie, but wrap it in kindness.
ex_zh: 一个粗糙的真相仍然比一个圆滑的谎言要好，但要用善意包裹它。

def_en: Sudden and unexpected; brief to the point of rudeness
quote_en: The meeting came to an abrupt end when the fire alarm rang.
quote_zh: 火警响起时，会议突然结束。
ex_en: An abrupt change can be frightening, but it can also be the jolt that wakes you from a dreamless sleep.
ex_zh: 突然的变化可能令人恐惧，但它也可能是将你从无梦的睡眠中唤醒的震动。

def_en: Complete and total; not limited in any way
quote_en: The dictator demanded absolute obedience from his citizens.
quote_zh: 独裁者要求公民绝对服从。
ex_en: Absolute certainty is a rare luxury; most of life is lived in the gray areas between.
ex_zh: 绝对确定是一种罕见的奢侈；大部分生活都是在灰色地带中度过的。

def_en: To restrain oneself from doing something
quote_en: The doctor advised him to abstain from alcohol for a month.
quote_zh: 医生建议他戒酒一个月。
ex_en: To abstain from judging others is the first step toward understanding them.
ex_zh: 戒除评判他人是理解他们的第一步。

def_en: To use wrongly or harmfully; cruel treatment
quote_en: It is wrong to abuse your power just because no one is watching.
quote_zh: 仅仅因为没人看见就滥用权力是错误的。
ex_en: Abuse of any kind — of power, of trust, of a person — leaves scars that outlast the act.
ex_zh: 任何形式的滥用——权力、信任、他人——都会留下比行为本身更长久的伤疤。

def_en: To make more noticeable or prominent
quote_en: The dark frame accentuates the bright colors of the painting.
quote_zh: 深色画框突出了画作的明亮色彩。
ex_en: Accentuate the positive, not to ignore problems, but to give yourself strength to solve them.
ex_zh: 突出积极的一面，不是为了忽视问题，而是为了给自己解决问题的力量。

def_en: An award or privilege granted as a special honor
quote_en: The actor received the highest accolade his industry could offer.
quote_zh: 那位演员获得了行业内最高荣誉。
ex_en: The greatest accolade is not a trophy, but a life that others are grateful to have known.
ex_zh: 最大的荣誉不是奖杯，而是别人感激曾经认识你的人生。

quote_en: She is an accomplished pianist who has played in many concerts.
quote_zh: 她是一位在许多音乐会上演奏过的有成就的钢琴家。
ex_en: An accomplished person is not one who never fails, but one who fails forward.
ex_zh: 一个有成就的人不是从不失败的人，而是向前失败的人。

def_en: Passive acceptance or agreement without protest
quote_en: His silence was taken as acquiescence to the plan.
quote_zh: 他的沉默被理解为对计划的默许。
ex_en: Acquiescence to injustice is the quiet accomplice of tyranny.
ex_zh: 对不公的默许是暴政的安静同谋。

def_en: Something obtained, especially a valuable addition
quote_en: The museum's latest acquisition is a rare 15th-century manuscript.
quote_zh: 博物馆最新获得的是珍贵的15世纪手稿。
ex_en: The most valuable acquisition is not a thing, but a skill or a friend.
ex_zh: 最有价值的获得不是一件东西，而是一项技能或一个朋友。

quote_en: The author wrote an addendum to clarify a point in the book.
quote_zh: 作者写了附录来澄清书中的一点。
ex_en: Every life has an addendum — the unwritten chapter that begins when you think the story is over.
ex_zh: 每个人生都有一个附录——当你以为故事已经结束时开始的那未写的一章。

quote_en: The teacher admonished the students for talking during the test.
quote_zh: 老师告诫学生们不要在考试时说话。
ex_en: Admonish yourself before you admonish others; the log in your own eye is harder to see.
ex_zh: 在告诫别人之前先告诫自己；自己眼中的梁木更难看见。

def_en: The arrival of a notable event or thing
quote_en: The advent of the internet changed how we communicate forever.
quote_zh: 互联网的出现永远改变了我们的沟通方式。
ex_en: With the advent of each new day comes a chance to rewrite your story.
ex_zh: 随着每一天的到来，都有一次重写你故事的机会。

quote_en: The two athletes were fierce adversaries on the court but friends off it.
quote_zh: 两位运动员在球场上是激烈的对手，场下却是朋友。
ex_en: Your greatest adversary is not the person who opposes you, but the fear that lives inside you.
ex_zh: 你最大的对手不是反对你的人，而是你内心的恐惧。

def_en: To officially attach or connect to an organization
quote_en: Our local club is affiliated with the national sports association.
quote_zh: 我们的本地俱乐部隶属于国家体育协会。
ex_en: Do not affiliate your identity with any single group; you are more than a label.
ex_zh: 不要将你的身份与任何单一群体联系起来；你不仅仅是一个标签。

quote_en: The witness affirmed that she had seen the accident.
quote_zh: 目击者肯定她看到了事故。
ex_en: Affirm the good in others often; it costs you nothing and grows everything.
ex_zh: 常常肯定他人身上的优点；它不花你分文，却让一切成长。

def_en: To attach or stick something to something else
quote_en: Please affix a stamp to the envelope before mailing it.
quote_zh: 邮寄前请在信封上贴上邮票。
ex_en: Affix your attention to what truly matters, and the rest will find its place.
ex_zh: 把你的注意力附在真正重要的事情上，其余的事情自会找到位置。

def_en: Something that causes pain or suffering
quote_en: The disease was a terrible affliction for the entire village.
quote_zh: 这种疾病对整个村庄来说是一场可怕的折磨。
ex_en: An affliction can be a teacher in disguise, if you let it sharpen rather than break you.
ex_zh: 痛苦可以是伪装的老师，如果你让它磨砺你而不是击垮你。

def_en: To suffer extreme worry or pain
quote_en: She agonized over whether to accept the job offer.
quote_zh: 她为是否接受那份工作而痛苦挣扎。
ex_en: Do not agonize over what you cannot change; use that energy to change what you can.
ex_zh: 不要为你无法改变的事情痛苦；用那份精力去改变你能改变的。

def_en: Causing great physical or mental pain
quote_en: The wait for the test results was agonizing.
quote_zh: 等待测试结果的过程令人痛苦。
ex_en: An agonizing choice is still a choice; make it, then make it right.
ex_zh: 一个令人痛苦的选择仍然是选择；做出它，然后让它正确。

def_en: To make someone feel isolated or estranged
quote_en: His constant criticism alienated even his closest friends.
quote_zh: 他不断的批评甚至疏远了他最亲密的朋友。
ex_en: To alienate others with arrogance is to build a prison around your own heart.
ex_zh: 用傲慢疏远他人，就是在自己的心周围建造一座监狱。

def_en: Able to use both hands equally well
quote_en: The ambidextrous pitcher could throw with either arm.
quote_zh: 那位双手灵巧的投手能用任何一只手臂投球。
ex_en: An ambidextrous mind sees both sides of an argument before choosing a side.
ex_zh: 一个双手灵巧的头脑在选边之前能看到争论的双方。

def_en: Having mixed feelings about someone or something
quote_en: She felt ambivalent about moving to a new city — excited but also scared.
quote_zh: 她对搬到一个新城市感到矛盾——既兴奋又害怕。
ex_en: Ambivalence is not weakness; it is the mind's honest recognition that life is rarely black and white.
ex_zh: 矛盾不是软弱；它是头脑诚实地认识到生活很少是非黑即白。

quote_en: The divorce was amicable; they remained friends afterward.
quote_zh: 离婚是友好的；他们之后仍然是朋友。
ex_en: An amicable disagreement is possible when respect outweighs the need to win.
ex_zh: 当尊重超过获胜的欲望时，友好的分歧是可能的。

quote_en: The amorous couple held hands and gazed at the sunset.
quote_zh: 那对恋人牵着手凝视着日落。
ex_en: An amorous heart is not measured by grand gestures, but by daily kindnesses.
ex_zh: 多情的心不是用宏大的姿态衡量的，而是用日常的善意。

def_en: A short, amusing story about a real event
quote_en: He told an anecdote about getting lost on his first day of school.
quote_zh: 他讲了一个关于上学第一天迷路的趣闻。
ex_en: An anecdote can teach more than a lecture, because laughter opens the door to learning.
ex_zh: 一个趣闻比一堂课教得更多，因为欢笑打开了学习之门。

def_en: To cause someone to become hostile
quote_en: Do not antagonize your brother by teasing him.
quote_zh: 不要通过取笑来激怒你的兄弟。
ex_en: To antagonize is easy; to calm is a skill worth learning.
ex_zh: 激怒很容易；平息是值得学习的技能。

def_en: Occurring before a war, especially the American Civil War
quote_en: The plantation house was built in the antebellum era.
quote_zh: 那座种植园房屋建于战前时期。
ex_en: Antebellum ideals often ignore the injustices that led to war; learn the whole story.
ex_zh: 战前的理想常常忽略导致战争的不公；了解整个故事。

def_en: Something that came before and influenced later events
quote_en: The antecedent of the conflict was a misunderstanding years ago.
quote_zh: 这场冲突的前因是多年前的一个误会。
ex_en: Every effect has an antecedent; trace your habits back to their root.
ex_zh: 每一个结果都有前因；把你的习惯追溯到它们的根源。

def_en: To precede in time; to date before the actual date
quote_en: The fossil antedates any known human settlement in the area.
quote_zh: 这块化石早于该地区任何已知的人类定居点。
ex_en: Do not antedate your worries; tomorrow's problems do not need today's attention.
ex_zh: 不要提前你的担忧；明天的问题不需要今天的关注。

def_en: A small room leading to a larger one
quote_en: We waited in the anteroom until the doctor was ready.
quote_zh: 我们在前厅等候直到医生准备好。
ex_en: Life has many anterooms; patience is the key that opens the next door.
ex_zh: 人生有许多前厅；耐心是打开下一扇门的钥匙。

def_en: A disappointing end to an exciting series of events
quote_en: After all the hype, the movie's ending was an anticlimax.
quote_zh: 经过所有炒作之后，电影的结局令人失望。
ex_en: An anticlimax is not a failure; it is a reminder that the journey matters more than the destination.
ex_zh: 虎头蛇尾不是失败；它提醒我们旅程比目的地更重要。

def_en: A remedy that counteracts poison; anything that relieves a harmful condition
quote_en: Laughter is often an antidote to stress.
quote_zh: 笑声常常是压力的解毒剂。
ex_en: Kindness is the antidote to much of the world's cruelty; carry it with you.
ex_zh: 善意是世界许多残酷的解毒剂；随身携带它。

quote_en: She felt an antipathy toward the new student for no clear reason.
quote_zh: 她对新生产生了一种没有明确原因的反感。
ex_en: Antipathy often grows from ignorance; meet the person, not the label.
ex_zh: 反感常常源于无知；去认识那个人，而不是标签。

def_en: A substance that prevents infection; clean and sterile
quote_en: The nurse applied antiseptic to the wound before bandaging it.
quote_zh: 护士在包扎前给伤口涂了消毒剂。
ex_en: An antiseptic environment may be safe, but a little healthy dirt builds immunity — in gardens and in life.
ex_zh: 无菌环境可能是安全的，但一点健康的泥土能增强免疫力——在花园里和在生活中都是如此。

def_en: Harmful to society; avoiding social interaction
quote_en: The teenager's antisocial behavior included skipping school and vandalism.
quote_zh: 那个青少年的反社会行为包括逃学和破坏公物。
ex_en: Antisocial silence is not the same as solitude that nourishes the soul.
ex_zh: 反社会的沉默与滋养灵魂的独处不同。

def_en: An antibody that neutralizes a toxin
quote_en: The doctor administered antitoxin to treat the snakebite.
quote_zh: 医生使用抗毒素治疗蛇咬伤。
ex_en: The antitoxin to hatred is not more hatred, but understanding.
ex_zh: 仇恨的抗毒素不是更多的仇恨，而是理解。

def_en: To make a serious request; to be attractive
quote_en: The charity appealed for donations to help flood victims.
quote_zh: 慈善机构呼吁捐款帮助洪水灾民。
ex_en: An appeal to someone's better nature is never wasted, even if it seems to fail.
ex_zh: 对某人良善本性的呼吁永远不会浪费，即使看似失败。

def_en: To assess the value or quality of something
quote_en: The jeweler appraised the diamond ring at five thousand dollars.
quote_zh: 珠宝商评估那枚钻石戒指价值五千美元。
ex_en: Appraise your own actions before you judge others; the mirror is harder to face than the window.
ex_zh: 在评判他人之前先评估自己的行为；镜子比窗户更难面对。

def_en: A person learning a trade from a skilled employer
quote_en: The young apprentice watched carefully as the master carved the wood.
quote_zh: 年轻的学徒仔细观察大师雕刻木头。
ex_en: Every master was once an apprentice who refused to quit.
ex_zh: 每一位大师都曾是一个拒绝放弃的学徒。

quote_en: She showed an early aptitude for mathematics.
quote_zh: 她早期就显示出数学天赋。
ex_en: Aptitude is a gift; what you do with it is your gift to the world.
ex_zh: 天赋是礼物；你用它做什么是你给世界的礼物。

quote_en: His honesty and kindness are his greatest assets.
quote_zh: 他的诚实和善良是他最大的资产。
ex_en: The only assets that cannot be taken from you are your character and your knowledge.
ex_zh: 唯一无法从你身上夺走的资产是你的品格和你的知识。

quote_en: The explosion tore the old building asunder.
quote_zh: 爆炸将旧建筑炸成碎片。
ex_en: A single lie can tear trust asunder; guard your words like precious jewels.
ex_zh: 一个谎言就能将信任撕裂成碎片；像守护珍贵珠宝一样守护你的言语。

def_en: The use of machines to do work previously done by people
quote_en: Automation has replaced many factory jobs with robots.
quote_zh: 自动化已经用机器人取代了许多工厂工作。
ex_en: Automation can free human hands, but it cannot free the human heart from the need to care.
ex_zh: 自动化可以解放人类的双手，但它无法将人类的心从关爱的需要中解放出来。

def_en: A feeling of wonder and respect mixed with fear
quote_en: The children gazed in awe at the starry night sky.
quote_zh: 孩子们敬畏地凝视着繁星点点的夜空。
ex_en: Awe is the doorway to humility; walk through it often.
ex_zh: 敬畏是通往谦逊的大门；常常走过它。

def_en: A confused noise of many voices
quote_en: The room was a babel of shouting and laughter.
quote_zh: 房间里充满了喊叫和笑声的嘈杂声。
ex_en: In the babel of opinions, listen for the quiet voice of truth.
ex_zh: 在众说纷纭的嘈杂中，倾听真理安静的声音。

def_en: To pester persistently; a burrowing animal
quote_en: The children badgered their parents to buy them ice cream.
quote_zh: 孩子们纠缠父母给他们买冰淇淋。
ex_en: Do not badger someone for an answer they are not ready to give; patience is a form of respect.
ex_zh: 不要纠缠别人给出他们还没准备好给出的答案；耐心是一种尊重。

quote_en: The friends exchanged good-natured banter throughout the meal.
quote_zh: 朋友们在整餐饭中互相开着善意的玩笑。
ex_en: Banter is the seasoning of friendship, but too much can spoil the dish.
ex_zh: 开玩笑是友谊的调味品，但太多会破坏这道菜。

quote_en: The basilica's dome towered over the city skyline.
quote_zh: 大教堂的圆顶高耸于城市天际线之上。
ex_en: A basilica is built stone by stone; a life of integrity is built choice by choice.
ex_zh: 大教堂是一块石头一块石头建成的；正直的人生是一个选择一个选择建成的。

def_en: A stronghold or fortress; a defender of a cause
quote_en: The castle was the last bastion of resistance against the invaders.
quote_zh: 这座城堡是抵抗入侵者的最后堡垒。
ex_en: Kindness is a bastion that no enemy can breach, for it disarms before the battle begins.
ex_zh: 善意是一座任何敌人都无法攻破的堡垒，因为它在战斗开始之前就解除了武装。

def_en: Cruel or unpleasant; like a beast
quote_en: The beastly weather ruined our plans for a picnic.
quote_zh: 恶劣的天气毁了我们野餐的计划。
ex_en: A beastly temper is a sign not of strength, but of a heart that has forgotten how to be gentle.
ex_zh: 野兽般的脾气不是力量的标志，而是一颗忘记了如何温柔的心。

quote_en: The hall was bedecked with flowers and ribbons for the party.
quote_zh: 大厅被鲜花和彩带装饰起来准备派对。
ex_en: Do not bedeck yourself with borrowed achievements; your own small steps are more honest.
ex_zh: 不要用借来的成就装饰自己；你自己的小步伐更诚实。

quote_en: The bull went berserk and charged at the fence.
quote_zh: 公牛狂暴起来冲向栅栏。
ex_en: Do not go berserk over what you cannot control; breathe, then act.
ex_zh: 不要为你无法控制的事情而狂暴；呼吸，然后行动。

quote_en: I beseech you to reconsider your decision.
quote_zh: 我恳求你重新考虑你的决定。
ex_en: To beseech the universe for change is to pray; to beseech yourself is to begin.
ex_zh: 恳求宇宙改变是祈祷；恳求自己是开始。

quote_en: The committee holds biannual meetings in spring and autumn.
quote_zh: 委员会在春季和秋季举行一年两次的会议。
ex_en: Biannual reflection on your goals can keep you from drifting off course.
ex_zh: 一年两次反思你的目标可以防止你偏离航向。

quote_en: The town celebrated its bicentennial with a week-long festival.
quote_zh: 小镇以长达一周的节日庆祝其二百周年纪念。
ex_en: A bicentennial reminds us that what lasts is built not by haste, but by steady hands over time.
ex_zh: 二百周年纪念提醒我们，持久的东西不是匆忙建成的，而是随着时间的推移由稳健的双手建成的。

quote_en: The siblings bickered constantly over who would use the remote.
quote_zh: 兄妹们不断为谁用遥控器而争吵。
ex_en: To bicker over small things is to waste the precious currency of time.
ex_zh: 为小事争吵就是浪费宝贵的时间货币。

quote_en: The biennial art exhibition attracts artists from around the world.
quote_zh: 两年一次的艺术展吸引了来自世界各地的艺术家。
ex_en: A biennial event gives you two years to prepare; do not wait until the last month.
ex_zh: 两年一次的活动给你两年的准备时间；不要等到最后一个月。

quote_en: The two countries signed a bilateral trade agreement.
quote_zh: 两国签署了一项双边贸易协定。
ex_en: A bilateral relationship succeeds when both sides give more than they take.
ex_zh: 当双方给予的多于索取时，双边关系就会成功。

quote_en: The bilingual student translated for her parents at the parent-teacher conference.
quote_zh: 那位双语学生在家长会上为父母翻译。
ex_en: To be bilingual is to have two windows on the same world; each opens a different view.
ex_zh: 掌握双语是在同一个世界上拥有两扇窗户；每一扇都打开不同的风景。

def_en: A large naturally occurring community of plants and animals
quote_en: The desert biome is characterized by very little rainfall.
quote_zh: 沙漠生物群系的特点是降雨量极少。
ex_en: Your home is a small biome; make it hospitable to kindness.
ex_zh: 你的家是一个小小的生物群系；让它对善意友好。

def_en: Irreverent speech or action against sacred things
quote_en: In some religions, speaking against God is considered blasphemy.
quote_zh: 在某些宗教中，反对上帝的言论被视为亵渎。
ex_en: The greatest blasphemy is not questioning faith, but using faith to justify cruelty.
ex_zh: 最大的亵渎不是质疑信仰，而是用信仰为残忍辩护。

def_en: Damaged or spoiled; suffering from disease
quote_en: The blighted crops yielded almost nothing that year.
quote_zh: 那年枯萎的庄稼几乎颗粒无收。
ex_en: A blighted hope can still be replanted; seeds do not know they are supposed to be dead.
ex_zh: 一个枯萎的希望仍然可以重新种植；种子不知道它们本该死了。

def_en: Relating to cows; slow and dull
quote_en: The bovine creatures grazed peacefully in the meadow.
quote_zh: 那些牛科动物在草地上平静地吃草。
ex_en: A bovine acceptance of injustice is the enemy of progress; question what seems wrong.
ex_zh: 对不公的迟钝接受是进步的敌人；质疑看起来错误的事情。

quote_en: The brawny firefighter carried the injured child down the ladder.
quote_zh: 那个强壮的消防员抱着受伤的孩子爬下梯子。
ex_en: Brawny arms can lift heavy loads, but a tender heart can lift a heavy spirit.
ex_zh: 强壮的手臂可以举起重物，但温柔的心可以提振沉重的精神。

def_en: A gap or break; to break through
quote_en: The water rushed through the breach in the dam.
quote_zh: 水通过大坝的缺口冲了进来。
ex_en: A breach of trust is like a cracked mirror; it can be repaired, but the scar remains.
ex_zh: 信任的裂痕就像一面破碎的镜子；它可以修复，但痕迹仍在。

quote_en: The bulbous nose of the clown was painted bright red.
quote_zh: 小丑球茎状的鼻子被涂成了鲜红色。
ex_en: A bulbous ego is a heavy burden; humility is lighter than air.
ex_zh: 球茎状膨胀的自我是沉重的负担；谦逊比空气还轻。

def_en: To persuade by flattery or gentle urging
quote_en: She cajoled her little brother into sharing his candy.
quote_zh: 她哄骗她的小弟弟分享他的糖果。
ex_en: To cajole is to use charm as a tool; be sure the tool serves a good purpose.
ex_zh: 哄骗是用魅力作为工具；确保工具服务于好的目的。

def_en: A disguise that blends with the surroundings
quote_en: The soldier's uniform provided excellent camouflage in the forest.
quote_zh: 士兵的制服在森林中提供了极好的伪装。
ex_en: Many flaws are camouflaged by busyness; stillness reveals them.
ex_zh: 许多缺点被忙碌伪装起来；静止揭示它们。

quote_en: The capillaries deliver oxygen to every cell in the body.
quote_zh: 毛细血管将氧气输送到身体的每一个细胞。
ex_en: Kindness is like a capillary; small, but without it, the whole system fails.
ex_zh: 善意就像毛细血管；微小，但没有它，整个系统就会失败。

quote_en: The ancient citadel stood on a hill overlooking the town.
quote_zh: 古老的城堡矗立在山丘上俯瞰小镇。
ex_en: Your mind is a citadel; guard what enters its gates.
ex_zh: 你的思想是一座城堡；守护进入它大门的东西。

def_en: Of the highest quality; a work of enduring excellence
quote_en: The novel is a classic of English literature.
quote_zh: 这部小说是英国文学的经典。
ex_en: A classic is not a classic because it is old, but because it speaks to every generation.
ex_zh: 经典之所以是经典，不是因为年代久远，而是因为它与每一代人对话。

def_en: To split apart; to cling to
quote_en: The axe cleaved the log into two pieces.
quote_zh: 斧头将圆木劈成两半。
ex_en: Do not cleave to the past so tightly that you have no hands free for the future.
ex_zh: 不要紧紧地依附于过去，以至于没有空手去拥抱未来。

quote_en: The clergy led the congregation in prayer.
quote_zh: 神职人员带领会众祈祷。
ex_en: The clergy serve the spirit; each of us can serve the spirit of kindness without a title.
ex_zh: 神职人员服务精神；我们每个人都可以在没有头衔的情况下服务善良的精神。

def_en: To force someone to do something against their will
quote_en: The kidnapper tried to coerce the family into paying a ransom.
quote_zh: 绑架者试图胁迫家人支付赎金。
ex_en: You can coerce compliance, but you cannot coerce loyalty or love.
ex_zh: 你可以强制服从，但你无法强制忠诚或爱。

quote_en: A cohesive team works better than a group of individuals.
quote_zh: 一个凝聚的团队比一群个体工作得更好。
ex_en: A cohesive community is not one without disagreements, but one where disagreements do not break the bond.
ex_zh: 一个凝聚的社区不是没有分歧的社区，而是分歧不会打破纽带的社区。

quote_en: She discussed the project with her colleagues at the meeting.
quote_zh: 她在会议上与同事们讨论了项目。
ex_en: A colleague is not just a coworker; a true colleague is a partner in purpose.
ex_zh: 同事不仅仅是共事者；真正的同事是目标上的伙伴。

quote_en: The comely cottage was surrounded by rose bushes.
quote_zh: 那座清秀的小屋被玫瑰丛环绕。
ex_en: A comely appearance may open doors, but only a comely character keeps them open.
ex_zh: 清秀的外表可能打开门，但只有清秀的品格才能让门保持敞开。

def_en: To force or drive someone to do something
quote_en: The evidence was so strong that it compelled a guilty verdict.
quote_zh: 证据如此有力，以至于迫使法庭做出了有罪判决。
ex_en: You cannot compel respect; you can only earn it.
ex_zh: 你无法强迫尊重；你只能赢得它。

def_en: Interest calculated on initial principal and accumulated interest
quote_en: Thanks to compound interest, her savings grew faster over time.
quote_zh: 由于复利，她的储蓄随着时间增长得更快。
ex_en: Compound interest applies to kindness too; one good deed multiplies when it inspires another.
ex_zh: 复利也适用于善意；一个善举在激励另一个时就会倍增。

quote_en: The final exam was comprehensive, covering everything from the whole year.
quote_zh: 期末考试是全面的，涵盖了一整年的所有内容。
ex_en: A comprehensive understanding of a problem is the first step toward solving it.
ex_zh: 对问题的全面理解是解决它的第一步。

quote_en: The concave mirror focused the light into a single point.
quote_zh: 凹面镜将光线聚焦到一个点上。
ex_en: A concave heart bends inward; a convex heart reaches outward to others.
ex_zh: 凹的心向内弯曲；凸的心向外伸向他人。

def_en: To prove something to be false
quote_en: The scientist confuted the old theory with new evidence.
quote_zh: 科学家用新证据驳倒了旧理论。
ex_en: To confute an error is valuable, but to replace it with truth is more valuable.
ex_zh: 驳倒一个错误是有价值的，但用真理取代它更有价值。

quote_en: The gravy began to congeal as it cooled.
quote_zh: 肉汁冷却时开始凝结。
ex_en: Hatred congeals the heart; forgiveness keeps it flowing.
ex_zh: 仇恨使心凝结；宽恕让它流动。

quote_en: Traffic congestion is worse during rush hour.
quote_zh: 交通拥堵在高峰时段更严重。
ex_en: Congestion in your schedule leaves no room for the unexpected joys of life.
ex_zh: 日程的拥堵为生活中意外的喜悦留不下空间。

def_en: Deliberately created rather than natural; artificial
quote_en: The plot of the movie felt contrived and unrealistic.
quote_zh: 电影的情节感觉做作而不真实。
ex_en: A contrived smile is worse than a genuine frown; honesty never goes out of style.
ex_zh: 做作的微笑比真诚的皱眉更糟糕；诚实永远不会过时。

quote_en: The convex lens magnified the tiny print.
quote_zh: 凸透镜放大了微小的印刷字体。
ex_en: A convex heart bulges with generosity; it gives more than it holds.
ex_zh: 一颗凸起的心因慷慨而鼓胀；它给予的比它持有的多。

def_en: A person who sends goods to be sold
quote_en: The consigner shipped the artwork to the auction house.
quote_zh: 托运人将艺术品运送到拍卖行。
ex_en: You are the consigner of your own actions; send only what you would be proud to receive.
ex_zh: 你是自己行动的托运人；只发送你收到会感到自豪的东西。

def_en: A person or institution to whom money is owed
quote_en: The company paid all its creditors before distributing profits.
quote_zh: 公司在分配利润之前支付了所有债权人。
ex_en: Time is a creditor that always collects; spend your hours wisely.
ex_zh: 时间是一个总是收取债务的债权人；明智地花费你的时间。

def_en: Relating to the customs and arts of a society
quote_en: The festival showcased the cultural traditions of many countries.
quote_zh: 节日展示了许多国家的文化传统。
ex_en: A cultural exchange is successful when both sides leave with more respect than they arrived with.
ex_zh: 文化交流的成功在于双方离开时带着比来时更多的尊重。

quote_en: The basement was dark and dank, with a smell of mold.
quote_zh: 地下室又暗又湿，有一股霉味。
ex_en: A dank spirit of gloom can be aired out by the sunlight of gratitude.
ex_zh: 阴湿的阴郁精神可以被感恩的阳光晾干。

def_en: Marked with spots or patches of light and shade
quote_en: Sunlight filtered through the leaves, creating a dappled pattern on the ground.
quote_zh: 阳光透过树叶，在地上形成了斑驳的图案。
ex_en: Life is dappled with both shadow and light; do not curse the shadow, for it proves the light is real.
ex_zh: 人生是斑驳的，既有阴影也有光；不要诅咒阴影，因为它证明光是真实的。

def_en: An entry recording a sum owed; to remove money from an account
quote_en: The purchase was debited directly from her bank account.
quote_zh: 这笔购买直接从她的银行账户扣除。
ex_en: Every unkind word is a debit from the account of trust; make more deposits than withdrawals.
ex_zh: 每一句不友善的话都是信任账户的借方；存款多于取款。

def_en: Worn out or ruined because of age or neglect
quote_en: The decrepit barn collapsed in the storm.
quote_zh: 那座破旧的谷仓在暴风雨中倒塌了。
ex_en: A decrepit excuse is a sign of a mind that has stopped growing.
ex_zh: 一个破旧的借口是一颗停止成长的心的标志。

def_en: Failure to fulfill an obligation; a preset option
quote_en: If you do not choose a setting, the computer will use the default.
quote_zh: 如果你不选择设置，电脑将使用默认设置。
ex_en: Do not default to cruelty when you are hurt; default to silence until you can speak with kindness.
ex_zh: 当你受伤时，不要默认选择残忍；默认选择沉默，直到你能用善意说话。

quote_en: The defiant teenager refused to follow the rules.
quote_zh: 那个挑衅的少年拒绝遵守规则。
ex_en: A defiant spirit can be noble when it defies injustice, not when it defies wisdom.
ex_zh: 一个挑衅的精神在反抗不公时是高尚的，而不是在反抗智慧时。

quote_en: The demise of the old regime made way for democracy.
quote_zh: 旧政权的终结为民主让路。
ex_en: Do not hasten the demise of a friendship over a misunderstanding; let patience work.
ex_zh: 不要因为一个误会而加速友谊的终结；让耐心发挥作用。

def_en: The act of treating something sacred with disrespect
quote_en: The vandalism of the cemetery was an act of desecration.
quote_zh: 对墓地的破坏是一种亵渎行为。
ex_en: Desecration of the truth is a crime against all who depend on it.
ex_zh: 对真相的亵渎是对所有依赖真相之人的犯罪。

def_en: A name or title; the act of assigning
quote_en: The official designation of the area as a park protected it from development.
quote_zh: 该地区被正式指定为公园，保护了它免受开发。
ex_en: Your designation does not define you; your actions do.
ex_zh: 你的头衔不定义你；你的行动定义你。

def_en: To feel contempt or strong dislike
quote_en: She despises people who lie to get what they want.
quote_zh: 她鄙视那些为了得到想要的东西而撒谎的人。
ex_en: To despise someone is to carry a heavy stone in your pocket; let it go.
ex_zh: 鄙视某人就是口袋里装着一块沉重的石头；放手吧。

def_en: In low spirits from loss of hope
quote_en: After failing the exam twice, he felt despondent.
quote_zh: 两次考试不及格后，他感到沮丧。
ex_en: Despondent moments are not the end of the story; they are the dark before the dawn.
ex_zh: 沮丧的时刻不是故事的结局；它们是黎明前的黑暗。

def_en: To destroy or overwhelm with shock
quote_en: The hurricane devastated the coastal town.
quote_zh: 飓风摧毁了沿海小镇。
ex_en: A harsh word can devastate a heart more than a storm can destroy a house.
ex_zh: 一句伤人的话比一场风暴摧毁房屋更能摧毁一颗心。

def_en: To recognize or show a difference
quote_en: It is hard to differentiate between the two twins.
quote_zh: 很难区分那对双胞胎。
ex_en: Differentiate between what you can change and what you cannot; then act accordingly.
ex_zh: 区分你能改变的和不能改变的；然后相应地行动。

def_en: A feeling of contempt; to scorn
quote_en: He looked at the messy room with disdain.
quote_zh: 他轻蔑地看着凌乱的房间。
ex_en: Disdain for another is often a mask for insecurity about oneself.
ex_zh: 对他人的鄙视常常是对自身不安全感的面具。

quote_en: The unfair pay raise disgruntled many employees.
quote_zh: 不公平的加薪使许多员工不满。
ex_en: A disgruntled heart finds fault everywhere; a grateful heart finds blessing everywhere.
ex_zh: 一个不满的心到处找茬；一个感恩的心到处找到祝福。

quote_en: The spy disguised himself as a janitor to avoid detection.
quote_zh: 间谍伪装成看门人以避免被发现。
ex_en: Kindness in disguise is still kindness; do not wait for recognition to give it.
ex_zh: 伪装的善意仍然是善意；不要等待认可才给予它。

quote_en: The old book's pages disintegrated when touched.
quote_zh: 那本旧书的书页一碰就碎了。
ex_en: Trust, once broken, does not disintegrate all at once; it crumbles one small betrayal at a time.
ex_zh: 信任一旦破碎，不会一下子解体；它是一次一次小小的背叛逐渐瓦解的。

quote_en: There is a wide disparity in wealth between the rich and the poor.
quote_zh: 富人和穷人之间存在巨大的贫富差距。
ex_en: A disparity in opinion is not a problem; a disparity in respect is.
ex_zh: 意见的差异不是问题；尊重的差异才是。

def_en: The act of cutting apart for study; detailed analysis
quote_en: The biology class performed a dissection of a frog.
quote_zh: 生物课对一只青蛙进行了解剖。
ex_en: Dissection of a mistake is painful, but it is the only way to learn not to repeat it.
ex_zh: 解剖一个错误是痛苦的，但这是学会不重复它的唯一方法。

quote_en: Her dogged effort finally paid off when she finished the marathon.
quote_zh: 她顽强的努力终于得到了回报，她完成了马拉松。
ex_en: A dogged spirit does not guarantee success, but it guarantees you will not quit before the finish line.
ex_zh: 一个顽强的精神不能保证成功，但它保证你不会在终点线之前放弃。

def_en: To have power or control over
quote_en: One large company dominates the market for smartphones.
quote_zh: 一家大公司主导了智能手机市场。
ex_en: Do not let fear dominate your decisions; let hope have a seat at the table.
ex_zh: 不要让恐惧支配你的决定；让希望在桌边有一个座位。

quote_en: The office walls were painted a drab gray.
quote_zh: 办公室的墙壁被漆成单调的灰色。
ex_en: A drab day can be brightened by a single act of unexpected kindness.
ex_zh: 一个单调的日子可以被一次意想不到的善意行为照亮。

quote_en: He hated the drudgery of washing dishes every night.
quote_zh: 他讨厌每天晚上洗碗的苦工。
ex_en: Drudgery becomes meaningful when you remember who benefits from your effort.
ex_zh: 当你记住谁从你的努力中受益时，苦工就有了意义。

quote_en: His egotistical behavior made him unpopular with teammates.
quote_zh: 他自我中心的行为使他在队友中不受欢迎。
ex_en: An egotistical person builds a throne and then finds no one to sit beside them.
ex_zh: 一个自我中心的人建造了一个王座，然后发现没有人坐在他们旁边。

def_en: To remove or get rid of completely
quote_en: The new filter eliminates almost all impurities from the water.
quote_zh: 新过滤器几乎消除了水中的所有杂质。
ex_en: Eliminate the word "can't" from your inner vocabulary; replace it with "how can I?"
ex_zh: 从你的内心词汇中消除“不能”这个词；用“我怎样才能”代替它。

def_en: Oval-shaped; using cryptic or ambiguous language
quote_en: The planet's orbit is elliptical, not a perfect circle.
quote_zh: 行星的轨道是椭圆形的，而不是完美的圆形。
ex_en: An elliptical answer may hide more than it reveals; clarity is a form of respect.
ex_zh: 一个隐晦的答案隐藏的可能比揭示的更多；清晰是一种尊重。

def_en: Famous and respected within a field
quote_en: The eminent scientist was invited to speak at the conference.
quote_zh: 那位杰出的科学家被邀请在会议上发言。
ex_en: An eminent title is earned, not claimed; let others call you eminent, never yourself.
ex_zh: 杰出的头衔是挣来的，不是自称的；让别人称你为杰出，永远不要自己说。

def_en: The production and release of something, especially gas
quote_en: Car emissions are a major cause of air pollution.
quote_zh: 汽车尾气是空气污染的主要原因。
ex_en: The emission of kind words into a tense room can change everything.
ex_zh: 在紧张的房间里释放善意的话语可以改变一切。

def_en: To attract and delight; to put under a spell
quote_en: The beautiful music enchanted everyone in the audience.
quote_zh: 美妙的音乐迷住了观众中的每一个人。
ex_en: Do not let material things enchant you so much that you forget the people who love you.
ex_zh: 不要让物质的东西如此迷惑你，以至于你忘记了爱你的人。

quote_en: The tour encompasses all the major sights of the city.
quote_zh: 这次旅行包含了城市的所有主要景点。
ex_en: A good leader's vision encompasses not only success but also the well-being of every team member.
ex_zh: 一个好的领导者的愿景不仅包含成功，也包含每个团队成员的福祉。

quote_en: The heavy backpack was an encumbrance during the hike.
quote_zh: 沉重的背包在徒步旅行中是个累赘。
ex_en: A grudge is an encumbrance you carry for someone else's mistake; drop it.
ex_zh: 怨恨是你为别人的错误背负的累赘；放下它。

quote_en: Adding herbs enhances the flavor of the soup.
quote_zh: 添加香草可以提升汤的味道。
ex_en: A kind word enhances any conversation more than the most clever insult.
ex_zh: 一句善意的话语比最聪明的侮辱更能提升任何对话。

def_en: To make certain something will happen
quote_en: Please ensure that all doors are locked before leaving.
quote_zh: 请确保离开前所有门都锁好了。
ex_en: You cannot ensure that you will never fail, but you can ensure that you will never stop trying.
ex_zh: 你无法确保你永远不会失败，但你可以确保你永远不会停止尝试。

quote_en: The vaccine helped eradicate smallpox from the world.
quote_zh: 疫苗帮助从世界上根除了天花。
ex_en: To eradicate a bad habit, you must replace it with a good one, not just remove it.
ex_zh: 要根除一个坏习惯，你必须用一个好习惯代替它，而不仅仅是移除它。

def_en: The gradual wearing away of something
quote_en: Wind and water cause the erosion of mountains over millions of years.
quote_zh: 风和水利造成山脉数百万年的侵蚀。
ex_en: The erosion of trust happens one small betrayal at a time; rebuild it one honest act at a time.
ex_zh: 信任的侵蚀是一次又一次微小的背叛发生的；一次一次诚实的行动重建它。

def_en: The tube that carries food from mouth to stomach
quote_en: Food passes from the throat down the esophagus to the stomach.
quote_zh: 食物从喉咙经过食道进入胃。
ex_en: The esophagus of communication is listening; without it, words cannot reach the heart.
ex_zh: 沟通的食道是倾听；没有它，话语无法到达心灵。

def_en: To cause someone to be no longer close or affectionate
quote_en: His constant criticism estranged him from his children.
quote_zh: 他不断的批评使他与孩子们疏远。
ex_en: To estrange yourself from your own conscience is to sail without a compass.
ex_zh: 与自己的良知疏远就是没有指南针的航行。

def_en: The customary code of polite behavior
quote_en: Good etiquette requires you to say "thank you" when someone helps you.
quote_zh: 良好的礼仪要求当有人帮助你时说“谢谢”。
ex_en: Etiquette is not about rules; it is about making others feel at ease.
ex_zh: 礼仪不是关于规则；而是关于让他人感到自在。

quote_en: Humans evolved from earlier primates over millions of years.
quote_zh: 人类在数百万年间从早期的灵长类动物进化而来。
ex_en: Your understanding of right and wrong should evolve as you grow; stay curious, not stuck.
ex_zh: 你对是非的理解应该随着你的成长而进化；保持好奇，不要停滞。

quote_en: Arguing only exacerbated the conflict between them.
quote_zh: 争吵只会加剧他们之间的冲突。
ex_en: Worrying exacerbates your problems; action, even small action, begins to solve them.
ex_zh: 担忧加剧你的问题；行动，即使是很小的行动，开始解决它们。

def_en: To dig out and remove earth or fossils
quote_en: Archaeologists excavated the site and found ancient tools.
quote_zh: 考古学家挖掘了遗址并发现了古代工具。
ex_en: Excavate the layers of your own assumptions; you might find treasure buried beneath.
ex_zh: 挖掘你自己假设的层层堆积；你可能会发现埋藏在其下的宝藏。

quote_en: She excels in mathematics and science.
quote_zh: 她擅长数学和科学。
ex_en: To excel is not to be better than others, but to be better than you were yesterday.
ex_zh: 擅长不是比别人好，而是比昨天的自己好。

def_en: To carry out a plan; to put to death
quote_en: The general executed the battle plan with precision.
quote_zh: 将军精确地执行了作战计划。
ex_en: A brilliant idea poorly executed is worth less than a modest idea executed well.
ex_zh: 一个执行糟糕的好主意不如一个执行良好的普通主意有价值。

quote_en: The company's expansion into new markets was successful.
quote_zh: 公司向新市场的扩张是成功的。
ex_en: Expansion of the heart is more valuable than expansion of the wallet.
ex_zh: 心的扩张比钱包的扩张更有价值。

def_en: A person living outside their native country
quote_en: The expatriate community in Paris holds monthly social events.
quote_zh: 巴黎的外籍人士社区每月举行社交活动。
ex_en: An expatriate learns that home is not a place, but the people who know your name.
ex_zh: 一个侨民学会家不是一个地方，而是知道你名字的人。

def_en: Convenient and practical, though possibly improper
quote_en: It was expedient to take the shortcut, even though it was against the rules.
quote_zh: 走捷径是权宜之计，尽管它违反了规则。
ex_en: The expedient choice is not always the right choice; short-term gain can mean long-term loss.
ex_zh: 权宜的选择不总是正确的选择；短期收益可能意味着长期损失。

quote_en: The student was expelled for cheating on the final exam.
quote_zh: 那名学生因在期末考试中作弊被开除。
ex_en: You cannot expel a bad habit without inviting a good one to take its place.
ex_zh: 你无法在不邀请一个好习惯取代它的位置的情况下驱逐一个坏习惯。

def_en: Covering a large area or range
quote_en: The fire caused extensive damage to the forest.
quote_zh: 火灾对森林造成了广泛的破坏。
ex_en: Extensive reading is like a slow rain that soaks deep into the soil of your mind.
ex_zh: 广泛的阅读就像一场慢慢渗入你心灵土壤的细雨。

quote_en: The pesticide exterminated the entire colony of ants.
quote_zh: 杀虫剂消灭了整个蚂蚁群。
ex_en: You cannot exterminate fear by ignoring it; you must face it and let it pass.
ex_zh: 你不能通过忽视恐惧来消灭它；你必须面对它并让它过去。

quote_en: Dinosaurs became extinct millions of years ago.
quote_zh: 恐龙在数百万年前灭绝了。
ex_en: A dream only becomes extinct when you stop feeding it.
ex_zh: 一个梦想只有在你停止滋养它时才会灭绝。

def_en: The farthest point; a severe degree
quote_en: The extremities of the body — fingers and toes — are most vulnerable to frostbite.
quote_zh: 身体的末端——手指和脚趾——最容易冻伤。
ex_en: In extremity of hardship, the human spirit often reveals its greatest strength.
ex_zh: 在苦难的极端情况下，人类精神常常展现出它最伟大的力量。

quote_en: The exuberant puppy ran in circles around the yard.
quote_zh: 精力充沛的小狗在院子里绕圈跑。
ex_en: Exuberant joy is contagious; spread it without counting the cost.
ex_zh: 精力充沛的喜悦是会传染的；不计代价地传播它。

def_en: To search obsessively; a small weasel-like animal
quote_en: The detective ferreted out the truth from a pile of lies.
quote_zh: 侦探从一堆谎言中搜寻出了真相。
ex_en: Ferret out the good in every person; it is there, even if buried deep.
ex_zh: 在每个人身上搜寻出优点；它就在那里，即使埋得很深。

quote_en: The fervid speech inspired the crowd to action.
quote_zh: 那场热情洋溢的演讲激励了人群行动起来。
ex_en: Fervid belief without evidence is dangerous; temper passion with reason.
ex_zh: 没有证据的炽热信念是危险的；用理性调和激情。

quote_en: The fickle weather shifted from sunshine to rain in an hour.
quote_zh: 善变的天气在一小时内从晴转雨。
ex_en: A fickle friend is like a fair-weather sail; useless in a storm.
ex_zh: 一个善变的朋友就像晴天的帆；暴风雨中毫无用处。

def_en: Able to bend or adapt easily
quote_en: The flexible schedule allowed her to work from home some days.
quote_zh: 灵活的时间安排让她有时可以在家工作。
ex_en: A flexible mind outlasts a rigid one, because it bends instead of breaking.
ex_zh: 一个灵活的心智比僵化的心智更持久，因为它弯曲而不是折断。

def_en: To search for food or provisions
quote_en: The bears foraged for berries in the forest.
quote_zh: 熊在森林里搜寻浆果。
ex_en: Forage for wisdom in unexpected places — a conversation, a mistake, a quiet moment.
ex_zh: 在意想不到的地方搜寻智慧——一次对话、一个错误、一个安静的时刻。

def_en: To take possession of property due to unpaid debt
quote_en: The bank threatened to foreclose on their home.
quote_zh: 银行威胁要取消他们房屋的赎回权。
ex_en: Do not foreclose on a friendship because of one disagreement; give it room to recover.
ex_zh: 不要因为一次分歧就取消友谊的赎回权；给它恢复的空间。

def_en: Inspiring fear or respect through being large or powerful
quote_en: The formidable opponent had never lost a match.
quote_zh: 那个强大的对手从未输过一场比赛。
ex_en: A formidable challenge is an invitation to grow; do not run from it.
ex_zh: 一个强大的挑战是成长的邀请；不要逃避它。

quote_en: The fury of the storm uprooted ancient trees.
quote_zh: 风暴的狂怒连根拔起了古老的树木。
ex_en: Fury is a fire that burns the house of the angry, not the target of their anger.
ex_zh: 狂怒是一场烧毁愤怒者自己房子的火，而不是他们愤怒的目标。

quote_en: The movie garnered several awards at the film festival.
quote_zh: 那部电影在电影节上获得了多个奖项。
ex_en: You garner respect not by demanding it, but by acting respectably.
ex_zh: 你不是通过要求获得尊重，而是通过行为得体来获得尊重。

def_en: All the people born around the same time; the act of producing
quote_en: Each generation faces its own unique challenges.
quote_zh: 每一代人都面临着自己独特的挑战。
ex_en: The greatest gift one generation can give the next is not wealth, but wisdom.
ex_zh: 一代人能给予下一代的最伟大礼物不是财富，而是智慧。

quote_en: Eye color is determined by genetic factors.
quote_zh: 眼睛颜色由遗传因素决定。
ex_en: Your genetic inheritance is not your destiny; your choices write the final chapter.
ex_zh: 你的遗传继承不是你的命运；你的选择书写最后一章。

quote_en: The genial host made everyone feel welcome.
quote_zh: 和蔼的主人让每个人都感到受欢迎。
ex_en: A genial spirit costs nothing but buys the loyalty of everyone you meet.
ex_zh: 和蔼的精神不花分文，却能赢得你遇到的每个人的忠诚。

quote_en: The local gentry donated generously to the hospital.
quote_zh: 当地的绅士阶层慷慨地向医院捐款。
ex_en: True gentry is not measured by birth, but by behavior.
ex_zh: 真正的绅士不是由出身衡量的，而是由行为衡量的。

def_en: Relating to geometry; using simple shapes like lines and circles
quote_en: The floor was decorated with geometric patterns of tiles.
quote_zh: 地板上装饰着几何图案的瓷砖。
ex_en: A geometric proof is beautiful because each step follows logically from the last; so should your arguments.
ex_zh: 几何证明之所以美，是因为每一步都逻辑地从前一步得出；你的论点也应如此。

def_en: To begin to grow; to sprout
quote_en: Seeds need water and warmth to germinate.
quote_zh: 种子需要水和温暖才能发芽。
ex_en: An idea must germinate in silence before it can bear fruit in action.
ex_zh: 一个想法在行动中结出果实之前必须在静默中发芽。

quote_en: Every human being deserves to be treated with dignity.
quote_zh: 每个人都应该被以尊严对待。
ex_en: To be human is to be imperfect; to grow is to learn from those imperfections.
ex_zh: 成为人就是不完美；成长是从这些不完美中学习。

quote_en: The humane treatment of prisoners is required by international law.
quote_zh: 国际法要求人道对待囚犯。
ex_en: A humane society is measured not by its prisons, but by how it treats its most vulnerable.
ex_zh: 一个人道的社会不是由它的监狱衡量的，而是由它如何对待最脆弱的人衡量的。

quote_en: The hyperactive puppy needed lots of exercise.
quote_zh: 那只过度活跃的小狗需要大量运动。
ex_en: A hyperactive mind can be a gift if you learn to focus its energy.
ex_zh: 如果你学会集中它的能量，一个过度活跃的头脑可以是一份礼物。

def_en: Exaggerated statements not meant to be taken literally
quote_en: Saying "I'm so hungry I could eat a horse" is hyperbole.
quote_zh: 说“我饿得能吃下一匹马”是夸张。
ex_en: Hyperbole can make a point memorable, but truth needs no exaggeration to be powerful.
ex_zh: 夸张可以使观点令人难忘，但真理不需要夸张就能有力。

def_en: The extension of a joint beyond its normal range
quote_en: The athlete suffered hyperextension of his knee during the game.
quote_zh: 那位运动员在比赛中膝盖过度伸展受伤。
ex_en: Hyperextension of a promise — stretching it beyond what you can deliver — leads to broken trust.
ex_zh: 承诺的过度伸展——将其拉伸到超出你能兑现的范围——导致信任破裂。

def_en: Relating to speeds five times the speed of sound or more
quote_en: Hypersonic missiles are extremely difficult to intercept.
quote_zh: 高超音速导弹极难拦截。
ex_en: Kindness travels at hypersonic speed; a single word can reach a heart in an instant.
ex_zh: 善意以高超音速传播；一个词可以瞬间到达一颗心。

quote_en: Hypertension can be managed with diet, exercise, and medication.
quote_zh: 高血压可以通过饮食、运动和药物来管理。
ex_en: Hypertension of the ego — an inflated sense of self — is a silent killer of relationships.
ex_zh: 自我的高血压——膨胀的自我意识——是关系的无声杀手。

def_en: A person who worries excessively about their health
quote_en: The hypochondriac visited the doctor for every minor ache.
quote_zh: 那位疑病症患者为每一个小疼痛去看医生。
ex_en: A hypochondriac of the soul worries constantly about imaginary moral failings; learn to trust your goodness.
ex_zh: 灵魂的疑病症患者不断担心想象中的道德失败；学会相信你的善良。

def_en: Pretending to have beliefs one does not actually hold
quote_en: The politician's hypocrisy was exposed when he was caught lying.
quote_zh: 那位政客的虚伪在他被抓到撒谎时暴露了。
ex_en: Hypocrisy is the tribute vice pays to virtue; it acknowledges that goodness is valuable, even while faking it.
ex_zh: 虚伪是恶行对美德支付的敬意；它承认善良是有价值的，即使是在假装它的时候。

def_en: Under the skin; relating to a syringe
quote_en: The nurse used a hypodermic needle to give the injection.
quote_zh: 护士用皮下注射针进行注射。
ex_en: A hypodermic of hope can revive a spirit faster than any medicine.
ex_zh: 一剂希望的精神皮下注射比任何药物都能更快地使精神复苏。

def_en: The longest side of a right triangle
quote_en: The hypotenuse is opposite the right angle in a triangle.
quote_zh: 斜边对着三角形中的直角。
ex_en: The shortest path between two points is a straight line; the hypotenuse of life's triangle is often the path of integrity.
ex_zh: 两点之间最短的路径是直线；人生三角形的斜边常常是正直之路。

quote_en: Let us consider a hypothetical situation where you won the lottery.
quote_zh: 让我们考虑一个假设的情况：你中了彩票。
ex_en: A hypothetical worry is a thief that steals from today without having earned the right.
ex_zh: 一个假设的担忧是一个没有赢得权利却从今天偷窃的小偷。

def_en: The process of starting a fire or engine
quote_en: He turned the key in the ignition and started the car.
quote_zh: 他把钥匙插进点火开关，发动了汽车。
ex_en: The ignition of a dream is a single spark of courage.
ex_zh: 梦想的点火是一点勇气的火花。

def_en: To involve deeply; to dip in liquid
quote_en: To learn Spanish quickly, immerse yourself in a Spanish-speaking country.
quote_zh: 要快速学习西班牙语，让自己沉浸在一个说西班牙语的国家。
ex_en: Immerse yourself in the joy of giving, and you will find that your own cup never empties.
ex_zh: 让自己沉浸在给予的喜悦中，你会发现自己的杯子永远不会空。

quote_en: The gods in Greek mythology were immortal.
quote_zh: 希腊神话中的神是不朽的。
ex_en: An act of kindness is immortal; it echoes long after the one who gave it is gone.
ex_zh: 一次善举是不朽的；它在给予者离开很久之后仍然回响。

quote_en: Drinking alcohol can impair your ability to drive safely.
quote_zh: 饮酒会损害你安全驾驶的能力。
ex_en: Do not impair your reputation by defending a wrong you know is wrong.
ex_zh: 不要通过捍卫你知道是错误的事情来损害你的声誉。

quote_en: The judge remained impassive as the verdict was read.
quote_zh: 宣读判决时，法官保持冷漠。
ex_en: An impassive face may hide a storm inside; do not judge calm as indifference.
ex_zh: 一张冷漠的脸可能隐藏着内心的风暴；不要把平静当作冷漠。

quote_en: The lost child implored the stranger for help.
quote_zh: 迷路的孩子恳求陌生人帮忙。
ex_en: Before you implore others for mercy, learn to grant it freely.
ex_zh: 在你恳求他人怜悯之前，学会自由地给予它。

quote_en: The family gathered for an impromptu picnic in the park.
quote_zh: 家人在公园里即兴举行了一次野餐。
ex_en: An impromptu act of kindness often feels more genuine than a planned one.
ex_zh: 一次即兴的善举往往比计划好的感觉更真诚。

quote_en: He had an impulse to buy the shoes, but decided to save his money.
quote_zh: 他有一种冲动想买那双鞋，但决定省钱。
ex_en: An impulse can be a whisper from your better self or a shout from your worst; learn which is which.
ex_zh: 一个冲动可以是你更好自我的低语，也可以是你最坏自我的呼喊；学会分辨。

quote_en: The shy student was often inarticulate during class discussions.
quote_zh: 那个害羞的学生在课堂讨论中常常口齿不清。
ex_en: An inarticulate heart can still feel deeply; listen to the silence between the stumbles.
ex_zh: 一个口齿不清的心仍然可以感受深刻；倾听结巴之间的沉默。

quote_en: The criminal faced a long incarceration for his crimes.
quote_zh: 罪犯因他的罪行面临长期监禁。
ex_en: Incarceration of the mind — refusing to learn — is a prison with no walls.
ex_zh: 思想的监禁——拒绝学习——是一座没有围墙的监狱。

quote_en: The outdoor event was canceled due to inclement weather.
quote_zh: 户外活动因恶劣天气取消。
ex_en: Inclement conditions test the strength of a shelter; inclement times test the strength of a character.
ex_zh: 恶劣的条件考验庇护所的力量；恶劣的时代考验品格的力量。

quote_en: The spy chose an inconspicuous spot to watch the meeting.
quote_zh: 间谍选了一个不显眼的地方观察会议。
ex_en: The most important acts of kindness are often inconspicuous — seen only by the giver and the receiver.
ex_zh: 最重要的善举往往是不显眼的——只有给予者和接受者看到。

quote_en: The players were indignant when the referee made a bad call.
quote_zh: 当裁判做出错误判罚时，球员们感到愤慨。
ex_en: Indignant outrage is easy; patient problem-solving is harder and more valuable.
ex_zh: 愤慨的愤怒很容易；耐心的解决问题更难也更有价值。

quote_en: Her indiscreet comment revealed confidential information.
quote_zh: 她不谨慎的评论泄露了机密信息。
ex_en: An indiscreet word cannot be recalled; pause before you let it fly.
ex_zh: 一个不谨慎的词无法召回；在让它飞出去之前暂停。

def_en: The process of reasoning from specific cases to general principles
quote_en: Through induction, the scientist concluded that all swans are white.
quote_zh: 通过归纳，科学家得出结论：所有天鹅都是白色的。
ex_en: Induction from one example is a trap; wait for more evidence before you judge.
ex_zh: 从一个例子归纳是一个陷阱；在你判断之前等待更多证据。

quote_en: Her influential book changed the way people think about education.
quote_zh: 她那本有影响力的书改变了人们对教育的看法。
ex_en: The most influential people are not those with the loudest voices, but those with the kindest hearts.
ex_zh: 最有影响力的人不是声音最大的人，而是心最善良的人。

def_en: Too small or unimportant to matter
quote_en: The error was insignificant and did not affect the result.
quote_zh: 错误是微不足道的，没有影响结果。
ex_en: No act of kindness is insignificant; it may be the only light someone sees that day.
ex_zh: 没有一次善举是微不足道的；它可能是某人那天看到的唯一的光。

quote_en: The students listened intently to the teacher's explanation.
quote_zh: 学生们专心地听老师的解释。
ex_en: Listen intently not only to words, but to the silence and the feelings beneath.
ex_zh: 不仅专心倾听话语，还要倾听话语下的沉默和感受。

quote_en: He is invariably late for morning meetings.
quote_zh: 他早上的会议总是迟到。
ex_en: What you practice invariably becomes who you are; choose your practices carefully.
ex_zh: 你练习的东西总会成为你是什么样的人；谨慎选择你的练习。

def_en: The act of turning something upside down or reversing order
quote_en: The inversion of "you are tired" becomes "tired are you" in poetic style.
quote_zh: 在诗歌风格中，“你很累”倒置成“累你很”。
ex_en: An inversion of perspective — seeing a problem from the other side — can reveal the solution.
ex_zh: 视角的倒置——从另一边看问题——可以揭示解决方案。

quote_en: The knight's armor made him nearly invulnerable in battle.
quote_zh: 骑士的盔甲使他在战斗中几乎无懈可击。
ex_en: No one is truly invulnerable; admitting vulnerability is a form of courage.
ex_zh: 没有人真正无懈可击；承认脆弱是一种勇气。

quote_en: It is irrational to be afraid of something that cannot hurt you.
quote_zh: 害怕不会伤害你的东西是不理性的。
ex_en: An irrational fear can rule your life if you never question its foundation.
ex_zh: 一个不理性的恐惧可以统治你的一生，如果你从不质疑它的基础。

def_en: Not capable of being saved or improved
quote_en: Some people believe that certain criminals are irredeemable.
quote_zh: 有些人认为某些罪犯是不可救药的。
ex_en: No person is truly irredeemable as long as they are willing to change.
ex_zh: 只要一个人愿意改变，就没有人是真正不可救药的。

quote_en: The evidence was irrefutable; he was guilty.
quote_zh: 证据无可辩驳；他有罪。
ex_en: An irrefutable fact does not always change a closed mind; be patient with those who need time.
ex_zh: 一个无可辩驳的事实并不总能改变封闭的思想；对需要时间的人要有耐心。

def_en: Not connected with the matter at hand
quote_en: His comments were irrelevant to the discussion.
quote_zh: 他的评论与讨论无关。
ex_en: An irrelevant worry is a thief that steals time from what matters.
ex_zh: 一个无关的担忧是从重要的事情上偷走时间的小偷。

def_en: Impossible to recover or put right
quote_en: The loss of the original manuscript was irretrievable.
quote_zh: 原始手稿的丢失是无法挽回的。
ex_en: Time lost is irretrievable; spend it on what you will not regret.
ex_zh: 失去的时间是无法挽回的；把它花在你不会后悔的事情上。

quote_en: The jovial baker always greeted customers with a smile.
quote_zh: 那位快乐的面包师总是微笑着迎接顾客。
ex_en: A jovial spirit is a magnet for friendship; be the one who brings warmth into a room.
ex_zh: 快乐的精神是友谊的磁铁；做那个把温暖带进房间的人。

def_en: A skilled worker who has completed apprenticeship
quote_en: After years as an apprentice, he became a journeyman electrician.
quote_zh: 做了多年学徒后，他成了一名熟练电工。
ex_en: Every master was once a journeyman who kept learning when no one was watching.
ex_zh: 每一位大师都曾是一个在没人注意时继续学习的熟练工。

quote_en: Muslims believe the Koran is the word of God revealed to Muhammad.
quote_zh: 穆斯林相信古兰经是启示给穆罕默德的真主之言。
ex_en: The Koran, like other sacred texts, has been used both to heal and to wound; read any holy book with humility.
ex_zh: 古兰经和其他经文一样，既被用来治愈也被用来伤害；以谦逊的态度阅读任何圣书。

def_en: Prepared according to Jewish dietary laws; proper
quote_en: The restaurant is certified kosher by a rabbi.
quote_zh: 这家餐厅由拉比认证为符合犹太教规。
ex_en: A kosher deal is not just legal; it is honest and fair.
ex_zh: 一个合规的交易不仅仅是合法的；它是诚实和公平的。

quote_en: The laborious task of cleaning the attic took all weekend.
quote_zh: 清理阁楼这项费力的任务花了整个周末。
ex_en: A laborious process often yields the most lasting results; shortcuts are not always shortcuts.
ex_zh: 一个费力的过程常常产生最持久的结果；捷径并不总是捷径。

quote_en: The dictator's lackeys carried out his every order without question.
quote_zh: 独裁者的走狗毫无疑问地执行他的每一个命令。
ex_en: Do not be a lackey to public opinion; think for yourself.
ex_zh: 不要做舆论的走狗；独立思考。

quote_en: The team's lackluster performance disappointed their fans.
quote_zh: 球队平淡的表现让球迷失望。
ex_en: A lackluster day can be brightened by a single act of unexpected kindness.
ex_zh: 一个平淡的日子可以被一次意想不到的善意行为照亮。

def_en: A person who falls behind others
quote_en: The laggard in the race finished an hour after the winner.
quote_zh: 比赛中的落后者在冠军之后一小时完赛。
ex_en: A laggard in learning is not a failure; they are just on their own timeline.
ex_zh: 学习上的落后者不是失败者；他们只是在走自己的时间线。

quote_en: The laity played an important role in the church's community work.
quote_zh: 平信徒在教会的社区工作中发挥了重要作用。
ex_en: The laity of any field — the non-experts — bring fresh eyes that experts sometimes lack.
ex_zh: 任何领域的非专业人士——外行——带来了专家有时缺乏的新鲜视角。

def_en: An official in the period between election and replacement
quote_en: The president became a lame duck after the election of his successor.
quote_zh: 总统在继任者当选后成为了跛脚鸭。
ex_en: A lame duck still has power; do not underestimate anyone just because their time is ending.
ex_zh: 一只跛脚鸭仍然有权力；不要仅仅因为一个人的时间快结束了就低估他们。

quote_en: The cartoonist lampooned the politician's latest scandal.
quote_zh: 漫画家讽刺了政客的最新丑闻。
ex_en: To lampoon is easy; to build up is harder. Choose the harder path.
ex_zh: 讽刺很容易；建设更难。选择更难的路。

quote_en: The larcenous employee stole office supplies for years.
quote_zh: 那个偷窃的员工多年来一直偷办公用品。
ex_en: A larcenous habit of stealing credit from others will eventually leave you alone.
ex_zh: 从别人那里偷取功劳的偷窃习惯最终会让你孤独。

def_en: The voice box; organ in the throat
quote_en: The singer damaged her larynx from overuse.
quote_zh: 歌手因过度使用损伤了喉。
ex_en: Your larynx is a tool; what you say with it is the art.
ex_zh: 你的喉是一个工具；你用它所说出的是艺术。

quote_en: The lascivious character in the novel was not admirable.
quote_zh: 小说中好色的角色并不令人钦佩。
ex_en: A lascivious gaze reduces a person to an object; see the whole person instead.
ex_zh: 好色的目光将人还原为物体；取而代之的是看到完整的人。

def_en: Having or showing excessive sexual desire
quote_en: The lecherous old man made inappropriate comments.
quote_zh: 那个好色的老人发表了不恰当的评论。
ex_en: A lecherous reputation is hard to wash off; guard your eyes and your words.
ex_zh: 好色的名声很难洗掉；守护你的眼睛和你的言语。

quote_en: The MRI showed a lesion on the patient's brain.
quote_zh: 核磁共振显示病人脑部有一个损伤。
ex_en: A lesion of the heart — a wound from betrayal — takes time to heal.
ex_zh: 心灵的损伤——来自背叛的伤口——需要时间来愈合。

quote_en: The snake's venom is lethal to small animals.
quote_zh: 蛇的毒液对小动物是致命的。
ex_en: A lethal word can kill a dream faster than any weapon.
ex_zh: 一句致命的话语比任何武器都能更快地杀死梦想。

quote_en: His bad temper is a liability in team projects.
quote_zh: 他的坏脾气在团队项目里是一个不利因素。
ex_en: A liability is not a flaw if you know it and manage it; self-awareness turns weaknesses into lessons.
ex_zh: 如果你知道并管理它，一个不利因素不是缺陷；自我意识将弱点变成教训。

quote_en: The liberation of the prisoners was celebrated across the country.
quote_zh: 囚犯的解放举国欢庆。
ex_en: The liberation of your mind from fear is a revolution that needs no army.
ex_zh: 你的思想从恐惧中解放是一场不需要军队的革命。

def_en: A legal claim against property as security for a debt
quote_en: The bank placed a lien on the house until the loan was repaid.
quote_zh: 银行对房屋设置了留置权直到贷款还清。
ex_en: A lien of guilt on your conscience will keep you from moving forward; pay the debt of apology.
ex_zh: 良心上的负罪留置权会阻止你前进；偿还道歉的债务。

def_en: A band of tissue connecting bones
quote_en: He tore a ligament in his knee during the soccer match.
quote_zh: 他在足球比赛中撕裂了膝盖的韧带。
ex_en: Trust is the ligament that holds relationships together; stretch it, but do not tear it.
ex_zh: 信任是将关系连接在一起的韧带；拉伸它，但不要撕裂它。

quote_en: The Lilliputian village was built as a model for the museum.
quote_zh: 那个极小的村庄是为博物馆做的模型。
ex_en: A Lilliputian act of kindness can have giant effects.
ex_zh: 一个极小的善举可以产生巨大的效果。

quote_en: He wrote a limerick about a cat who played the violin.
quote_zh: 他写了一首关于一只拉小提琴的猫的五行打油诗。
ex_en: A limerick can hide a serious truth behind its laughter.
ex_zh: 一首五行打油诗可以在笑声背后隐藏严肃的真理。

def_en: Arranged in a straight line; sequential
quote_en: The story followed a linear timeline from beginning to end.
quote_zh: 故事遵循从始至终的线性时间线。
ex_en: Life is not linear; expect detours and trust that they lead somewhere.
ex_zh: 人生不是线性的；期待绕行，并相信它们通向某个地方。

quote_en: The media lionized the young inventor as a genius.
quote_zh: 媒体将那位年轻发明家捧为天才。
ex_en: To lionize someone is to forget they are human; admire, but do not worship.
ex_zh: 崇拜某人就是忘记他们是人；欣赏，但不要崇拜。

quote_en: The loathsome crime shocked the entire community.
quote_zh: 那桩可憎的罪行震惊了整个社区。
ex_en: A loathsome act does not make a loathsome person beyond redemption.
ex_zh: 一个可憎的行为不会使一个可憎的人无可救药。

quote_en: The rainforest is known for its luxuriant vegetation.
quote_zh: 热带雨林以其繁茂的植被而闻名。
ex_en: A luxuriant imagination is a gift, but it must be watered with facts.
ex_zh: 繁茂的想象力是一份礼物，但它必须用事实浇灌。

quote_en: Cleaning the garage was a mammoth task.
quote_zh: 清理车库是一项巨大的任务。
ex_en: A mammoth challenge becomes manageable when broken into small pieces.
ex_zh: 一个巨大的挑战在分解成小块时就变得可以管理了。

quote_en: The villain laughed with maniacal glee.
quote_zh: 反派带着疯狂的喜悦大笑。
ex_en: A maniacal pursuit of a goal without ethics will destroy what you achieve.
ex_zh: 没有道德的疯狂追求目标会摧毁你所取得的成就。

def_en: A handwritten or typed document, especially an author's draft
quote_en: The author submitted her manuscript to the publisher.
quote_zh: 作者将她的手稿提交给出版商。
ex_en: Your life is a manuscript that you are writing every day; make each chapter worth reading.
ex_zh: 你的人生是你每天都在书写的手稿；让每一章都值得阅读。

quote_en: Marital problems can sometimes be resolved with counseling.
quote_zh: 婚姻问题有时可以通过咨询解决。
ex_en: Marital harmony is not the absence of conflict, but the presence of forgiveness.
ex_zh: 婚姻和谐不是没有冲突，而是有宽恕。

quote_en: The doctor extracted bone marrow for a transplant.
quote_zh: 医生提取骨髓用于移植。
ex_en: Laugh until you feel it in your marrow; joy should not stay on the surface.
ex_zh: 笑到你在骨髓里感受到它；喜悦不应停留在表面。

quote_en: The country was under martial law after the coup.
quote_zh: 政变后该国处于军事管制之下。
ex_en: A martial spirit is needed to fight injustice, but a peaceful spirit is needed to build what comes after.
ex_zh: 需要军事精神来对抗不公，但需要和平精神来建设之后的一切。

def_en: A place that attracts many people
quote_en: The city is a mecca for art lovers and collectors.
quote_zh: 这座城市是艺术爱好者和收藏家的圣地。
ex_en: A mecca of learning is not a place, but a state of mind that seeks truth everywhere.
ex_zh: 学习的圣地不是一个地方，而是一种到处寻求真理的心态。

def_en: To interfere in something that is not one's concern
quote_en: Do not meddle in other people's arguments unless asked.
quote_zh: 除非被请求，否则不要干涉别人的争论。
ex_en: To meddle is to assume you know better; sometimes the kindest action is to step back.
ex_zh: 干涉是假设你更了解；有时最善意的行动是退后一步。

quote_en: The meddlesome neighbor always gave unwanted advice.
quote_zh: 那个好干涉的邻居总是给出不想要的建议。
ex_en: A meddlesome tongue can undo more good than a helping hand can build.
ex_zh: 一条好干涉的舌头能毁掉的好事比一只帮助的手能建立的更多。

quote_en: The herb has medicinal qualities that reduce inflammation.
quote_zh: 这种草药具有减少炎症的药用特性。
ex_en: Laughter is medicinal without a prescription; take it daily.
ex_zh: 笑声是不需要处方的药用；每天服用。

quote_en: A sense of melancholy settled over him on rainy days.
quote_zh: 雨天一种忧郁感笼罩着他。
ex_en: Melancholy is not an illness; it is the soul's way of telling you to rest and reflect.
ex_zh: 忧郁不是一种疾病；它是灵魂告诉你休息和反思的方式。

quote_en: He took a menial job cleaning floors while looking for better work.
quote_zh: 他在寻找更好工作的同时做了一份清洁地板的卑微工作。
ex_en: No honest work is menial when done with dignity; the task does not define the person.
ex_zh: 当以尊严完成时，没有诚实的工作是卑微的；任务不定义人。

def_en: A seven-branched candelabrum used in Jewish worship
quote_en: The family lit the menorah each night of Hanukkah.
quote_zh: 那家人在光明节的每个夜晚点燃 menorah。
ex_en: A menorah of kindness has many branches; each small light pushes back a little darkness.
ex_zh: 善意的烛台有许多分支；每一小盏灯都驱散一点黑暗。

def_en: The chemical processes that maintain life
quote_en: Exercise can boost your metabolism and help you burn calories.
quote_zh: 运动可以提高你的新陈代谢并帮助你燃烧卡路里。
ex_en: The metabolism of learning — turning information into wisdom — requires time and digestion.
ex_zh: 学习的代谢——将信息转化为智慧——需要时间和消化。

def_en: Resembling metal in color, sound, or texture
quote_en: The car was painted a shiny metallic blue.
quote_zh: 汽车被漆成闪亮的金属蓝色。
ex_en: A metallic clang is harsh; a gentle word is music.
ex_zh: 金属的叮当声是刺耳的；温柔的话语是音乐。

quote_en: The crisis tested the mettle of the young leader.
quote_zh: 危机考验了那位年轻领导者的勇气。
ex_en: Mettle is not the absence of fear, but the decision to act despite it.
ex_zh: 勇气不是没有恐惧，而是不顾恐惧行动的决定。

def_en: To shape; a hollow container; a fungus
quote_en: The artist molded the clay into a beautiful vase.
quote_zh: 艺术家将粘土塑造成一个美丽的花瓶。
ex_en: You cannot mold a person into what they are not; you can only help them become what they already are.
ex_zh: 你无法将一个人塑造成他们不是的样子；你只能帮助他们成为他们已经是的样子。

def_en: Concerning right and wrong; a lesson
quote_en: The moral of the story is that honesty is the best policy.
quote_zh: 这个故事的寓意是诚实是最好的策略。
ex_en: A moral compass is useless if you never look at it.
ex_zh: 如果你从不看它，道德指南针就没有用。

def_en: The confidence and enthusiasm of a group
quote_en: The team's morale was high after their victory.
quote_zh: 胜利后团队的士气高涨。
ex_en: A single word of encouragement can lift morale more than a bonus.
ex_zh: 一句鼓励的话比奖金更能提振士气。

def_en: The state of being subject to death
quote_en: The doctor studied infant mortality rates in developing countries.
quote_zh: 医生研究了发展中国家的婴儿死亡率。
ex_en: Awareness of mortality is not morbid; it is the engine that makes each day precious.
ex_zh: 意识到死亡不是病态的；它是让每一天变得珍贵的引擎。

quote_en: The mortician prepared the body for the funeral service.
quote_zh: 殡仪业者为葬礼服务准备遗体。
ex_en: A mortician knows that the body is a vessel; what matters is what the vessel carried.
ex_zh: 殡仪业者知道身体是一个容器；重要的是容器承载了什么。

quote_en: The mosque's minaret towered above the neighborhood.
quote_zh: 清真寺的宣礼塔高耸于社区之上。
ex_en: A mosque, a church, a temple — all are houses for the same human longing for meaning.
ex_zh: 清真寺、教堂、寺庙——都是人类对意义的相同渴望的房屋。

def_en: A Muslim learned in religious law
quote_en: The mullah led the prayers at the mosque.
quote_zh: 毛拉在清真寺带领祈祷。
ex_en: A mullah, like any religious leader, is human; respect the position, but question with kindness.
ex_zh: 毛拉和任何宗教领袖一样，是人；尊重这个职位，但以善意质疑。

quote_en: The job involved mundane tasks like filing and copying.
quote_zh: 这份工作涉及归档和复印等平凡的任务。
ex_en: The mundane moments of life are not wasted; they are the fabric that holds the extraordinary together.
ex_zh: 生活中平凡的时刻没有被浪费；它们是将非凡维系在一起的织物。

quote_en: A genetic mutation caused the plant to have white flowers.
quote_zh: 基因突变导致植物开白花。
ex_en: A small mutation in your daily habits can cause a huge change in your future.
ex_zh: 日常习惯的一个微小突变可以导致你未来的巨大变化。

quote_en: His narcissism made him unable to see the needs of others.
quote_zh: 他的自恋使他看不到别人的需求。
ex_en: Narcissism is a mirror that shows only yourself; break it and see the world.
ex_zh: 自恋是一面只显示你自己的镜子；打破它，看见世界。

quote_en: The CEO was accused of nepotism for hiring his son.
quote_zh: 首席执行官因雇佣儿子被指责裙带关系。
ex_en: Nepotism may help in the short term, but merit builds lasting success.
ex_zh: 裙带关系可能在短期内有所帮助，但能力建立持久的成功。

quote_en: The antacid neutralized the acid in her stomach.
quote_zh: 抗酸剂中和了她胃里的酸。
ex_en: Kindness can neutralize anger better than any argument.
ex_zh: 善意比任何争论都能更好地中和愤怒。

quote_en: The comedian's jokes were offensive to many in the audience.
quote_zh: 那位喜剧演员的笑话冒犯了观众中的许多人。
ex_en: An offensive word can undo years of friendship; choose your words like you choose your battles.
ex_zh: 一个冒犯的词可以毁掉多年的友谊；像选择你的战斗一样选择你的言语。

quote_en: The tradition originated in ancient China.
quote_zh: 这个传统起源于古代中国。
ex_en: Do not let fear originate in your mind; plant hope instead.
ex_zh: 不要让恐惧在你的头脑中起源；改种希望。

def_en: Relating to the correction of bones or muscles
quote_en: The orthopedic surgeon repaired the broken bone.
quote_zh: 骨科医生修复了骨折。
ex_en: An orthopedic mindset — straightening what is crooked — applies to morals as well as bones.
ex_zh: 矫形的心态——矫正弯曲的东西——适用于道德也适用于骨骼。

quote_en: The patient's face was pallid after losing blood.
quote_zh: 失血后病人的脸很苍白。
ex_en: A pallid excuse is worse than a bold truth.
ex_zh: 一个苍白的借口比一个大胆的真相更糟糕。

quote_en: When the fire alarm rang, pandemonium broke out in the theater.
quote_zh: 火警响起时，剧院里一片喧嚣。
ex_en: In the pandemonium of the world, find a quiet corner of your own heart.
ex_zh: 在世界的喧嚣中，找到自己内心一个安静的角落。

quote_en: The parasitic plant took nutrients from the tree.
quote_zh: 寄生植物从树上获取养分。
ex_en: A parasitic relationship takes without giving; true friendship is mutual.
ex_zh: 寄生的关系只索取不给予；真正的友谊是相互的。

def_en: A loud ringing of bells; a burst of sound
quote_en: A peal of thunder shook the windows.
quote_zh: 一声雷声震动了窗户。
ex_en: A peal of laughter from a friend can be more healing than any medicine.
ex_zh: 朋友的一阵笑声比任何药物都更能治愈。

quote_en: The journey through the jungle was perilous.
quote_zh: 穿越丛林的旅程是危险的。
ex_en: A perilous path often leads to the most beautiful views.
ex_zh: 一条危险的路常常通向最美的风景。

quote_en: In the story, Death is personified as a hooded figure.
quote_zh: 在故事中，死亡被拟人化为一个戴兜帽的人物。
ex_en: Wisdom personified is not a statue; it is a friend who tells you when you are wrong.
ex_zh: 智慧的人格化不是一座雕像；它是一个在你犯错时告诉你的朋友。

def_en: To represent an abstract quality as a person
quote_en: The artist personified hope as a young woman with a lantern.
quote_zh: 艺术家将希望拟人化为一个提着灯笼的年轻女子。
ex_en: Personify patience when others rush; you will become a shelter for the frantic.
ex_zh: 当别人匆忙时成为耐心的化身；你将成为狂乱者的庇护所。

quote_en: The smell of fresh bread pervaded the entire house.
quote_zh: 新鲜面包的香味弥漫整个房子。
ex_en: Let kindness pervade your words and actions until it becomes your second nature.
ex_zh: 让善意弥漫在你的言语和行动中，直到它成为你的第二天性。

def_en: The form and size of a person's body
quote_en: The swimmer had a lean, muscular physique.
quote_zh: 那位游泳运动员有瘦削而肌肉发达的体格。
ex_en: A strong physique is a gift; a strong character is a choice.
ex_zh: 强壮的体格是礼物；坚强的品格是选择。

quote_en: One common pitfall of beginners is trying to do too much at once.
quote_zh: 初学者常见的一个陷阱是一次尝试做太多事情。
ex_en: The pitfall of success is believing you have nothing left to learn.
ex_zh: 成功的陷阱是相信你已经没有什么可学的了。

def_en: A flat shape with many straight sides
quote_en: A square is a polygon with four sides.
quote_zh: 正方形是一个有四条边的多边形。
ex_en: A polygon of many sides is like a person of many talents; both are stronger for their variety.
ex_zh: 一个多边的多边形就像一个多才多艺的人；两者都因多样性而更强。

def_en: An examination after death; an analysis after an event
quote_en: The postmortem revealed the cause of death was poisoning.
quote_zh: 尸检显示死因是中毒。
ex_en: A postmortem of a failed project is not for blame, but for learning.
ex_zh: 一个失败项目的事后分析不是为了责备，而是为了学习。

quote_en: The young athlete has the potential to become a champion.
quote_zh: 那位年轻运动员有成为冠军的潜力。
ex_en: Your potential is not a destination; it is a direction.
ex_zh: 你的潜力不是一个目的地；它是一个方向。

quote_en: The preponderance of evidence pointed to the defendant's guilt.
quote_zh: 压倒性的证据指向被告有罪。
ex_en: The preponderance of your time should go to what matters most, not what screams loudest.
ex_zh: 你大部分时间应该花在最重要的事情上，而不是叫得最响的事情上。

quote_en: The course is primarily for beginners, but advanced students can also join.
quote_zh: 这门课程主要面向初学者，但高年级学生也可以参加。
ex_en: Live primarily for others, and you will find yourself unexpectedly fulfilled.
ex_zh: 主要为他人而活，你会发现自己意外地得到满足。

def_en: To investigate deeply; a tool for exploration
quote_en: The doctor used a probe to examine the wound.
quote_zh: 医生用探针检查伤口。
ex_en: Probe your own motives before you judge the actions of others.
ex_zh: 在评判他人的行动之前，先探究自己的动机。

quote_en: The proliferation of smartphones has changed daily life.
quote_zh: 智能手机的激增改变了日常生活。
ex_en: The proliferation of kind words costs nothing but changes everything.
ex_zh: 善意话语的激增不花分文，却改变一切。

quote_en: The prominent scientist was interviewed on television.
quote_zh: 那位杰出的科学家在电视上接受了采访。
ex_en: A prominent nose does not make a prominent person; character does.
ex_zh: 一个突出的鼻子并不能造就一个杰出的人；品格才能。

def_en: To cause or encourage; done without delay
quote_en: The reminder prompted me to pay my bill on time.
quote_zh: 提醒促使我按时支付账单。
ex_en: A prompt apology can heal a wound before it scars.
ex_zh: 及时的道歉可以在伤口结疤之前愈合它。

quote_en: The strong wind propelled the sailboat across the lake.
quote_zh: 强风推动帆船穿过湖泊。
ex_en: Curiosity will propel you further than ambition ever will.
ex_zh: 好奇心比野心更能推动你前进。

def_en: A part or share of a whole; relative size
quote_en: A large proportion of the students passed the exam.
quote_zh: 很大比例的学生通过了考试。
ex_en: Keep your problems in proportion; most are smaller than they appear in the dark.
ex_zh: 保持你的问题在适当比例；大多数比它们在黑暗中看起来的要小。

def_en: The force that drives something forward
quote_en: The rocket's propulsion system lifted it into space.
quote_zh: 火箭的推进系统将其送入太空。
ex_en: The propulsion of a dream is not fuel, but the will to take the first step.
ex_zh: 梦想的推进力不是燃料，而是迈出第一步的意志。

quote_en: The nail protruded from the board and caught my sleeve.
quote_zh: 钉子从木板中突出，钩住了我的袖子。
ex_en: Do not let your ego protrude so far that others have to walk around it.
ex_zh: 不要让你的自我突出到别人不得不绕着它走的地步。

def_en: The act of supplying; a condition in a document
quote_en: The provision of clean water is essential for public health.
quote_zh: 提供清洁水对公共卫生至关重要。
ex_en: Make provision for future storms while the sun is still shining.
ex_zh: 在阳光还在照耀时为未来的风暴做好准备。

quote_en: Smoking can cause serious pulmonary diseases.
quote_zh: 吸烟会导致严重的肺部疾病。
ex_en: A pulmonary breath of fresh air can clear a cluttered mind.
ex_zh: 一口新鲜空气的肺部呼吸可以理清混乱的头脑。

def_en: The rhythmic beating of arteries; a regular beat
quote_en: The doctor checked the patient's pulse and breathing.
quote_zh: 医生检查了病人的脉搏和呼吸。
ex_en: The pulse of a community is not its economy, but its kindness.
ex_zh: 一个社区的脉搏不是它的经济，而是它的善意。

quote_en: The cold water quenched his thirst after the long run.
quote_zh: 冷水在长跑后解了他的渴。
ex_en: You cannot quench the thirst for meaning with possessions; only purpose satisfies.
ex_zh: 你无法用物质财富解渴对意义的渴望；只有目标才能满足。

def_en: A traitor who collaborates with an enemy
quote_en: The quisling was executed for betraying his country.
quote_zh: 那个卖国贼因背叛国家被处决。
ex_en: A quisling to your own values is a betrayal that leaves no scars on the outside.
ex_zh: 对自己价值观的背叛是一种不留下外在伤痕的背叛。

def_en: Bright and shining; full of joy
quote_en: The bride looked radiant on her wedding day.
quote_zh: 新娘在婚礼那天看起来光芒四射。
ex_en: A radiant smile costs nothing but can light up a room.
ex_zh: 一个光芒四射的微笑不花分文，却能照亮一个房间。

def_en: To search roughly or violently; to plunder
quote_en: The burglars ransacked the house, throwing everything onto the floor.
quote_zh: 窃贼洗劫了房子，把所有东西都扔到地板上。
ex_en: Do not ransack your memory for past hurts; let them lie where they belong.
ex_zh: 不要在你的记忆中翻找过去的伤痛；让它们待在它们该在的地方。

quote_en: He rashly quit his job before finding another one.
quote_zh: 他在找到另一份工作之前轻率地辞了职。
ex_en: A decision made rashly often has to be undone slowly.
ex_zh: 一个轻率做出的决定常常需要缓慢地撤销。

quote_en: During the war, each family received a ration of sugar and meat.
quote_zh: 战争期间，每个家庭得到糖和肉的配给。
ex_en: Ration your worries; most of what you fear never happens.
ex_zh: 定量你的担忧；你害怕的大多数事情从未发生。

quote_en: She rebuffed his offer of help with a sharp "no."
quote_zh: 她用尖锐的“不”拒绝了他的帮助提议。
ex_en: A rebuff is not a judgment of your worth; it is simply a closed door. Find another.
ex_zh: 一个拒绝不是对你价值的评判；它只是一扇关闭的门。找到另一扇。

quote_en: The recession caused many businesses to close.
quote_zh: 经济衰退导致许多企业倒闭。
ex_en: A recession of the spirit — a time of low energy — is not permanent; rest and renew.
ex_zh: 精神的衰退——一段低能量的时期——不是永久的；休息并更新。

def_en: To tell a story; to count again
quote_en: The survivor recounted her escape from the fire.
quote_zh: 幸存者讲述了她从火灾中逃生的经历。
ex_en: Recount your blessings daily; you will find they outnumber your troubles.
ex_zh: 每天讲述你的福气；你会发现它们多于你的烦恼。

quote_en: After surgery, she needed weeks of recuperation.
quote_zh: 手术后，她需要数周的恢复。
ex_en: Recuperation is not laziness; it is the body's wisdom demanding rest.
ex_zh: 恢复不是懒惰；它是身体要求休息的智慧。

def_en: The act of improving or changing
quote_en: The reformation of the prison system took decades.
quote_zh: 监狱系统的改革花了数十年。
ex_en: Personal reformation begins with one small honest look in the mirror.
ex_zh: 个人的改革始于一次对镜子的微小诚实的审视。

quote_en: The old theater was refurbished with new seats and lighting.
quote_zh: 旧剧院用新座位和照明进行了翻新。
ex_en: Refurbish your mind with new ideas; old thoughts can gather dust.
ex_zh: 用新思想翻新你的头脑；旧思想会积灰。

quote_en: Some lizards can regenerate their tails after losing them.
quote_zh: 一些蜥蜴在失去尾巴后可以再生。
ex_en: You can regenerate hope even after it seems dead; water it with small acts of courage.
ex_zh: 即使在看似死亡之后，你也可以再生希望；用小勇气浇灌它。

quote_en: Let me reiterate the main points of the presentation.
quote_zh: 让我重申一下演讲的要点。
ex_en: To reiterate a truth is not boring; some truths need to be heard many times before they sink in.
ex_zh: 重申一个真理并不无聊；有些真理需要被多次听到才能深入人心。

quote_en: She was reluctant to speak in front of a large crowd.
quote_zh: 她不愿意在一大群人面前讲话。
ex_en: A reluctant apology is better than none, but a heartfelt one is better still.
ex_zh: 一个不情愿的道歉比没有好，但一个发自内心的更好。

quote_en: The government voted to repeal the unpopular tax.
quote_zh: 政府投票废除了不受欢迎的税。
ex_en: You cannot repeal the consequences of your actions; you can only learn from them.
ex_zh: 你无法废除你行为的后果；你只能从中学习。

quote_en: The idea of hurting animals is repugnant to most people.
quote_zh: 伤害动物的想法对大多数人来说是令人厌恶的。
ex_en: What is repugnant to you may be normal to another; seek understanding before condemnation.
ex_zh: 令你厌恶的可能是别人的常态；在谴责之前寻求理解。

quote_en: The army repulsed the enemy's attack.
quote_zh: 军队击退了敌人的进攻。
ex_en: Do not repulse a kind gesture because you are afraid of being hurt; vulnerability is brave.
ex_zh: 不要因为害怕受伤而拒绝一个善意的姿态；脆弱是勇敢的。

quote_en: The queen was resplendent in her gold and diamond gown.
quote_zh: 女王穿着金色钻石礼服，光彩照人。
ex_en: A resplendent sunset reminds us that endings can be beautiful.
ex_zh: 一个灿烂的日落提醒我们，结局可以是美丽的。

def_en: The act of returning something to its original state
quote_en: The restoration of the ancient painting took two years.
quote_zh: 那幅古画的修复花了两年时间。
ex_en: The restoration of a broken friendship is one of life's greatest achievements.
ex_zh: 修复一段破裂的友谊是人生最伟大的成就之一。

def_en: The sale of goods to consumers
quote_en: The product retails for twenty dollars.
quote_zh: 该产品零售价为二十美元。
ex_en: Kindness is not for wholesale; it works best when given retail, person to person.
ex_zh: 善意不是批发的；它在一对一的零售中效果最好。

quote_en: The sadistic villain tortured his captives for fun.
quote_zh: 那个施虐的反派以折磨俘虏为乐。
ex_en: A sadistic comment reveals more about the speaker's pain than the target's flaw.
ex_zh: 一个施虐的评论揭示的更多是说话者的痛苦而非目标者的缺点。

quote_en: The church offered sanctuary to refugees.
quote_zh: 教堂为难民提供庇护。
ex_en: Your own mind can be a sanctuary or a battlefield; choose the thoughts you let in.
ex_zh: 你自己的头脑可以是一个避难所或一个战场；选择你让进来的想法。

def_en: A person blamed for the wrongdoings of others
quote_en: The manager looked for a scapegoat for the project's failure.
quote_zh: 经理为项目的失败寻找替罪羊。
ex_en: Blaming a scapegoat may relieve guilt, but it never solves the problem.
ex_zh: 责怪替罪羊可能缓解内疚，但它从不解决问题。

quote_en: The critic wrote a scathing review of the film.
quote_zh: 评论家写了一篇尖锐的电影评论。
ex_en: A scathing tongue can wound more deeply than any sword; heal with silence.
ex_zh: 尖锐的舌头比任何剑都能更深刻地伤害；用沉默治愈。

quote_en: The students scoffed at the idea of a dress code.
quote_zh: 学生们嘲笑着装规定的想法。
ex_en: Do not scoff at small beginnings; every oak was once an acorn.
ex_zh: 不要嘲笑微小的开始；每一棵橡树都曾是一颗橡子。

quote_en: She looked with scorn at the messy desk.
quote_zh: 她轻蔑地看着凌乱的桌子。
ex_en: Scorn is a heavy coat; take it off, and you will walk lighter.
ex_zh: 轻蔑是一件沉重的外套；脱掉它，你会走得更轻。

quote_en: The self-effacing hero refused all credit for his bravery.
quote_zh: 那位谦逊的英雄拒绝为自己勇敢的行为接受任何赞誉。
ex_en: A self-effacing spirit does not diminish your worth; it makes room for others to shine.
ex_zh: 谦逊的精神不会贬低你的价值；它为他人的闪耀腾出空间。

def_en: Priority based on length of service
quote_en: Promotions were based on seniority, not skill.
quote_zh: 晋升基于资历而非技能。
ex_en: Seniority deserves respect, but wisdom does not always come with age.
ex_zh: 资历值得尊重，但智慧并不总是随年龄而来。

quote_en: The serpentine road wound through the mountains.
quote_zh: 蜿蜒的道路穿过群山。
ex_en: A serpentine path may be longer, but it often shows you views you would otherwise miss.
ex_zh: 一条蜿蜒的路可能更长，但它常常展示你否则会错过的景色。

def_en: Embarrassed due to shame or shyness
quote_en: He gave a sheepish smile after being caught in a lie.
quote_zh: 被抓到撒谎后，他露出了羞愧的微笑。
ex_en: A sheepish admission of a mistake is braver than a proud denial.
ex_zh: 羞愧地承认错误比骄傲地否认更勇敢。

def_en: Sharp and clever, especially in business
quote_en: The shrewd investor made a fortune by buying early.
quote_zh: 那位精明的投资者通过早期购买发了财。
ex_en: Shrewdness without honesty is just clever theft.
ex_zh: 没有诚实的精明只是聪明的偷窃。

quote_en: The athlete stretched his sinews before the race.
quote_zh: 运动员在比赛前拉伸了肌腱。
ex_en: The sinew of a community is not its buildings, but the trust between its people.
ex_zh: 一个社区的筋骨不是它的建筑，而是人与人之间的信任。

quote_en: She was skeptical about the claim that the product worked instantly.
quote_zh: 她对产品立即见效的说法表示怀疑。
ex_en: A skeptical mind is a healthy mind, as long as it remains open to evidence.
ex_zh: 一个怀疑的头脑是健康的头脑，只要它对证据保持开放。

quote_en: The slovenly student's homework was always crumpled.
quote_zh: 那个邋遢学生的作业总是皱巴巴的。
ex_en: A slovenly mind is worse than a slovenly room; both can be cleaned.
ex_zh: 一个邋遢的头脑比一个邋遢的房间更糟糕；两者都可以清理。

quote_en: The cold weather made the snake sluggish.
quote_zh: 寒冷的天气使蛇行动迟缓。
ex_en: A sluggish start does not predict a slow finish; momentum builds.
ex_zh: 一个迟缓的开始并不能预示一个缓慢的结束；动力会积累。

quote_en: The snobbish waiter looked down on customers who didn't order wine.
quote_zh: 那个势利的服务员看不起不点酒的顾客。
ex_en: A snobbish attitude is a wall that keeps good people out and loneliness in.
ex_zh: 一种势利的态度是一堵墙，它把好人挡在外面，把孤独关在里面。

quote_en: The sociable puppy loved meeting new people.
quote_zh: 那只社交的小狗喜欢认识新朋友。
ex_en: Being sociable is not about talking; it is about making others feel welcome.
ex_zh: 社交不是关于说话；而是关于让他人感到受欢迎。

quote_en: The sophisticated computer could solve problems in seconds.
quote_zh: 那台精密的计算机能在几秒钟内解决问题。
ex_en: A sophisticated argument is not necessarily true; simplicity often houses deeper wisdom.
ex_zh: 一个复杂的论点不一定正确；简单常常蕴含着更深的智慧。

def_en: The state of being steady and unchanging
quote_en: The bridge's stability was tested by strong winds.
quote_zh: 桥梁的稳定性受到强风的考验。
ex_en: Emotional stability is not the absence of feelings, but the ability to ride them without drowning.
ex_zh: 情绪稳定不是没有感觉，而是能够在不被淹没的情况下驾驭它们。

quote_en: The statuesque model stood head and shoulders above the crowd.
quote_zh: 那位雕像般的模特比人群高出一个头。
ex_en: A statuesque posture is impressive, but a humble heart is unforgettable.
ex_zh: 雕像般的姿态令人印象深刻，但一颗谦逊的心令人难忘。

quote_en: The teacher used games to stimulate the students' interest.
quote_zh: 老师用游戏来激发学生们的兴趣。
ex_en: A good question stimulates more than a good answer.
ex_zh: 一个好问题比一个好答案更能激发思考。

quote_en: The stolid guard never reacted to the prisoners' taunts.
quote_zh: 那个冷漠的守卫对囚犯的嘲弄从不反应。
ex_en: A stolid exterior may hide a sensitive heart; do not mistake stillness for emptiness.
ex_zh: 一个冷漠的外表可能隐藏着一颗敏感的心；不要把静止当作空虚。

quote_en: Hiking up the mountain was a strenuous activity.
quote_zh: 徒步上山是一项费力的活动。
ex_en: A strenuous effort today makes tomorrow's challenges easier.
ex_zh: 今天的费力努力让明天的挑战更容易。

quote_en: The magician's final trick was stupendous.
quote_zh: 魔术师的最后一个把戏是惊人的。
ex_en: A stupendous achievement is built on thousands of unseen small efforts.
ex_zh: 一个惊人的成就是建立在成千上万看不见的小努力之上的。

def_en: Below the level of conscious thought
quote_en: His subconscious fear of heights made him avoid tall buildings.
quote_zh: 他对高处的潜意识恐惧使他避开高楼。
ex_en: Your subconscious believes what you repeatedly tell it; feed it kind words.
ex_zh: 你的潜意识相信你反复告诉它的话；用善意的话语喂养它。

def_en: To overcome or bring under control
quote_en: The police subdued the angry crowd.
quote_zh: 警察制服了愤怒的人群。
ex_en: Subdue your pride before it subdues your friendships.
ex_zh: 在你的骄傲制服你的友谊之前制服它。

def_en: The act of yielding to authority
quote_en: The general demanded the enemy's unconditional submission.
quote_zh: 将军要求敌人无条件投降。
ex_en: Submission to fear is a defeat; submission to wisdom is a victory.
ex_zh: 向恐惧屈服是失败；向智慧屈服是胜利。

quote_en: The subordinate officer followed the captain's orders.
quote_zh: 下属军官听从船长的命令。
ex_en: A subordinate role does not mean a subordinate mind; think for yourself.
ex_zh: 一个下属的角色并不意味着一个下属的头脑；独立思考。

quote_en: The assistant was too subservient to ever disagree.
quote_zh: 那个助手太屈从了，从不敢反对。
ex_en: A subservient spirit may keep peace, but it may also lose itself.
ex_zh: 一个屈从的精神可能维持和平，但它也可能失去自我。

quote_en: The government feared the subversion of the rebel group.
quote_zh: 政府害怕叛军组织的颠覆。
ex_en: The subversion of a bad idea is not treason; it is progress.
ex_zh: 对一个坏思想的颠覆不是叛国；它是进步。

quote_en: The teenager sat in sullen silence after being scolded.
quote_zh: 被责骂后，少年愠怒地沉默坐着。
ex_en: A sullen mood is a signal, not a sentence; ask yourself what it is protecting.
ex_zh: 一个愠怒的情绪是一个信号，不是判决；问问它在保护什么。

quote_en: The wound was superficial and healed quickly.
quote_zh: 伤口很浅，很快就愈合了。
ex_en: A superficial judgment of a person is like reading the first page and claiming to know the book.
ex_zh: 对一个人的肤浅判断就像读了第一页就声称知道整本书。
